message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Research web apps & infra">
You are a research specialist on team "harbor-research" (lead: "team-lead"). The codebase at D:\github\harbor is **Harbor** (v0.15.0) — a monorepo: Python CLI (`src/harbor/`), React viewer (`apps/viewer/`), Next.js docs (`docs/`), uv workspace packages (`packages/`).

YOUR TASK (#5): Survey Harbor's **web apps, storage, packages & CI/CD**. Read the actual code/config. Cover:
1. VIEWER APP: `apps/viewer/` — read its package.json + main entry. Tech stack (React Router + Vite per docs — verify), what it visualizes (trials/trajectories/rewards?), how it sources data (API endpoint? local files? a FastAPI server in `src/harbor/viewer/`?).
2. DOCS: `docs/` — Next.js + Fumadocs (verify from package.json), structure.
3. STORAGE & AUTH: `src/harbor/storage/` (Supabase), `src/harbor/db/`, `src/harbor/auth/` (OAuth callback server). Note the AGENTS.md rule about Supabase/PostgREST pagination (>1000 rows). Show how results are persisted/retrieved.
4. PUBLISHER & UPLOAD/DOWNLOAD: `src/harbor/publisher/` (package publishing & registry DB), `src/harbor/upload/`, `src/harbor/download/`.
5. PACKAGES (uv workspace): read root `pyproject.toml` `[tool.uv.workspace]` + `packages/` — enumerate members (harbor-rewardkit, harbor-langsmith), what each provides, how they're consumed.
6. CI/CD: `.github/workflows/` — enumerate workflows (pytest, ruff-format, ty, claude, claude-code-review, sync-registry, adapter-review, check-registry-format, pr-labeler, update-parity-summary) with one-line each from the YAML.
7. RL/TRAINING: `examples/training/` and the "RL optimization / rollout generation" claim — how Harbor produces rollouts for RL. `rfcs/` and `skills/` — brief overview.

Be efficient; cite paths. Flag discrepancies vs docs.

DELIVERABLE: Return as your **final message** a structured markdown summary: (a) viewer app overview (stack + what it shows + data source), (b) docs site, (c) storage/auth model + persistence flow, (d) publisher/upload/download, (e) workspace packages table, (f) CI/CD matrix table, (g) RL/training + rfcs/skills overview, (h) discrepancies. Then TaskUpdate task #5 to completed and SendMessage "team-lead" a 2-line teaser.
</teammate-message>

content.text: I'll conduct a comprehensive survey of Harbor's web apps, storage, packages, and CI/CD. Let me systematically explore each area.

1	[project]
2	name = "harbor"
3	version = "0.15.0"
4	description = "A framework for evaluating and optimizing agents and models using sandboxed environments."
5	readme = "README.md"
6	license = "Apache-2.0"
7	license-files = ["LICENSE"]
8	authors = [{ name = "Alex Shaw", email = "alexgshaw64@gmail.com" }]
9	requires-python = ">=3.12"
10	dependencies = [
11	    "pydantic>=2.11.7",
12	    "shortuuid>=1.0.13",
13	    "typer>=0.16.0",
14	    "requests>=2.32.4",
15	    "pyyaml>=6.0.2",
16	    "rich>=14.1.0",
17	    "toml>=0.10.2",
18	    "tenacity>=9.1.2",
19	    "python-dotenv>=1.1.1",
20	    "litellm>=1.83.14",
21	    "jinja2>=3.1.6",
22	    "datasets>=4.4.1",
23	    "dirhash>=0.5.0",
24	    "packaging>=25.0",
25	    "fastapi>=0.128.0",
26	    "uvicorn>=0.38.0",
27	    "pathspec>=1.0.3",
28	    "supabase>=2.28.2",
29	    "httpx>=0.27.0",
30	]
31	
32	[project.scripts]
33	harbor = "harbor.cli.main:app"
34	hr = "harbor.cli.main:app"
35	hb = "harbor.cli.main:app"
36	
37	[build-system]
38	requires = ["uv_build>=0.8.4,<0.9.0"]
39	build-backend = "uv_build"
40	
41	[tool.uv.workspace]
42	members = ["packages/*"]
43	
44	[tool.uv]
45	# tensorlake>=0.5.25 pins websockets<14, while langsmith[sandbox]>=0.8.8 needs
46	# websockets>=15 — an unsatisfiable conflict in the combined `all`/`cloud`
47	# extras. Force websockets>=15 so resolution succeeds; tensorlake's sandbox
48	# paths used by Harbor don't exercise the websockets API affected by the cap.
49	override-dependencies = ["websockets>=15"]
50	
51	[tool.uv.sources]
52	harbor-rewardkit = { workspace = true }
53	harbor-langsmith = { workspace = true }
54	
55	[project.optional-dependencies]
56	adapter = ["claude-agent-sdk>=0.1.17"]
57	langsmith = ["harbor-langsmith", "langsmith[sandbox]>=0.8.8"]
58	e2b = ["e2b>=2.25.0", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
59	daytona = ["daytona>=0.184.0"]
60	islo = ["islo>=0.3.3", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
61	modal = ["modal>=1.5.0"]
62	runloop = ["runloop-api-client>=1.23.2", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
63	tensorlake = ["tensorlake>=0.5.46"]
64	gke = ["kubernetes>=32.0.0"]
65	ec2 = ["boto3>=1.41.5"]
66	novita = ["novita-sandbox>=2.0.0a3", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
67	cwsandbox = ["cwsandbox>=0.23.3"]
68	wandb = ["wandb>=0.27", "cwsandbox>=0.23.3"]
69	use-computer = ["use-computer>=0.0.2"]
70	blaxel = ["blaxel>=0.2.52", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
71	# computer-1 native flavors use the vendor SDKs (anthropic[bedrock] brings
72	# boto3 for AnthropicBedrock). The generic litellm JSON harness needs no
73	# extra and remains the default-install fallback.
74	computer-1 = [
75	    "openai>=2.0",
76	    "anthropic[bedrock]>=0.102.0",
77	    "google-genai>=2.3.0",
78	]
79	cloud = ["harbor[cwsandbox]", "harbor[wandb]", "harbor[e2b]", "harbor[daytona]", "harbor[islo]", "harbor[modal]", "harbor[runloop]", "harbor[langsmith]", "harbor[gke]", "harbor[ec2]", "harbor[tensorlake]", "harbor[novita]", "harbor[use-computer]", "harbor[blaxel]"]
80	
81	all = ["harbor[cloud]", "harbor[tinker]", "harbor[computer-1]", "harbor[dspy]", "harbor[adapter]"]
82	tinker = [
83	    "tinker>=0.14.0",
84	    "tinker-cookbook>=0.1.0",
85	]
86	dspy = [
87	    "dspy>=2.6.0",
88	]
89	
90	[dependency-groups]
91	dev = [
92	    "harbor[cloud]",
93	    "harbor[tinker]",
94	    "harbor[computer-1]",
95	    "harbor[dspy]",
96	    "harbor[adapter]",
97	    "harbor-rewardkit[all]",
98	    "harbor-langsmith",
99	    "ipykernel>=6.30.1",
100	    "pytest>=8.4.2",
101	    "pytest-asyncio>=1.2.0",
102	    "pytest-cov>=7.0.0",
103	    "pytest-xdist>=3.8.0",
104	    "ruff>=0.15.17",
105	    "ty>=0.0.49",
106	    "hypothesis>=6.155.0",
107	]
108	
109	[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
110	known-first-party = ["harbor", "harbor_langsmith", "rewardkit"]
111	
112	[tool.pytest.ini_options]
113	testpaths = ["tests", "packages/rewardkit/tests", "packages/harbor-langsmith/tests"]
114	python_files = ["test_*.py"]
115	python_classes = ["Test*"]
116	python_functions = ["test_*"]
117	addopts = ["-v", "--tb=short", "--strict-markers", "--strict-config"]
118	markers = [
119	    "asyncio: marks tests as async tests",
120	    "runtime: marks tests as runtime tests (may need docker)",
121	    "unit: marks tests as unit tests (fast, no external dependencies)",
122	    "integration: marks tests as integration tests (requires external services, could be mocked)",
123	    "windows_containers: marks tests requiring Docker in Windows container mode",
124	]
125	asyncio_mode = "auto"
126	
127	[tool.coverage.run]
128	source = ["src/harbor", "packages/rewardkit/src/rewardkit", "packages/harbor-langsmith/src/harbor_langsmith"]
129	omit = ["*/tests/*", "*/__pycache__/*", "*/migrations/*"]
130	
131	[tool.coverage.report]
132	exclude_lines = [
133	    "pragma: no cover",
134	    "def __repr__",
135	    "if self.debug:",
136	    "if settings.DEBUG",
137	    "raise AssertionError",
138	    "raise NotImplementedError",
139	    "if 0:",
140	    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
141	]
142	
143	[tool.ty.environment]
144	python = ".venv"
145	
146	[tool.ty.rules]
147	all = "error"
148	[tool.ty.src]
149	include = ["src/harbor", "packages/rewardkit/src", "packages/harbor-langsmith/src"]
150	exclude = ["src/harbor/cli/template-adapter", "src/harbor/cli/template-task", "src/harbor/agents/installed/openhands_sdk_runner.py", "src/harbor/agents/installed/acp_runner.py", "src/harbor/agents/installed/nemo_agent_run_wrapper.py"]
151

[project]
name = "harbor"
version = "0.15.0"
description = "A framework for evaluating and optimizing agents and models using sandboxed environments."
readme = "README.md"
license = "Apache-2.0"
license-files = ["LICENSE"]
authors = [{ name = "Alex Shaw", email = "alexgshaw64@gmail.com" }]
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "pydantic>=2.11.7",
    "shortuuid>=1.0.13",
    "typer>=0.16.0",
    "requests>=2.32.4",
    "pyyaml>=6.0.2",
    "rich>=14.1.0",
    "toml>=0.10.2",
    "tenacity>=9.1.2",
    "python-dotenv>=1.1.1",
    "litellm>=1.83.14",
    "jinja2>=3.1.6",
    "datasets>=4.4.1",
    "dirhash>=0.5.0",
    "packaging>=25.0",
    "fastapi>=0.128.0",
    "uvicorn>=0.38.0",
    "pathspec>=1.0.3",
    "supabase>=2.28.2",
    "httpx>=0.27.0",
]

[project.scripts]
harbor = "harbor.cli.main:app"
hr = "harbor.cli.main:app"
hb = "harbor.cli.main:app"

[build-system]
requires = ["uv_build>=0.8.4,<0.9.0"]
build-backend = "uv_build"

[tool.uv.workspace]
members = ["packages/*"]

[tool.uv]
# tensorlake>=0.5.25 pins websockets<14, while langsmith[sandbox]>=0.8.8 needs
# websockets>=15 — an unsatisfiable conflict in the combined `all`/`cloud`
# extras. Force websockets>=15 so resolution succeeds; tensorlake's sandbox
# paths used by Harbor don't exercise the websockets API affected by the cap.
override-dependencies = ["websockets>=15"]

[tool.uv.sources]
harbor-rewardkit = { workspace = true }
harbor-langsmith = { workspace = true }

[project.optional-dependencies]
adapter = ["claude-agent-sdk>=0.1.17"]
langsmith = ["harbor-langsmith", "langsmith[sandbox]>=0.8.8"]
e2b = ["e2b>=2.25.0", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
daytona = ["daytona>=0.184.0"]
islo = ["islo>=0.3.3", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
modal = ["modal>=1.5.0"]
runloop = ["runloop-api-client>=1.23.2", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
tensorlake = ["tensorlake>=0.5.46"]
gke = ["kubernetes>=32.0.0"]
ec2 = ["boto3>=1.41.5"]
novita = ["novita-sandbox>=2.0.0a3", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
cwsandbox = ["cwsandbox>=0.23.3"]
wandb = ["wandb>=0.27", "cwsandbox>=0.23.3"]
use-computer = ["use-computer>=0.0.2"]
blaxel = ["blaxel>=0.2.52", "dockerfile-parse>=2.0.1"]
# computer-1 native flavors use the vendor SDKs (anthropic[bedrock] brings
# boto3 for AnthropicBedrock). The generic litellm JSON harness needs no
# extra and remains the default-install fallback.
computer-1 = [
    "openai>=2.0",
    "anthropic[bedrock]>=0.102.0",
    "google-genai>=2.3.0",
]
cloud = ["harbor[cwsandbox]", "harbor[wandb]", "harbor[e2b]", "harbor[daytona]", "harbor[islo]", "harbor[modal]", "harbor[runloop]", "harbor[langsmith]", "harbor[gke]", "harbor[ec2]", "harbor[tensorlake]", "harbor[novita]", "harbor[use-computer]", "harbor[blaxel]"]

all = ["harbor[cloud]", "harbor[tinker]", "harbor[computer-1]", "harbor[dspy]", "harbor[adapter]"]
tinker = [
    "tinker>=0.14.0",
    "tinker-cookbook>=0.1.0",
]
dspy = [
    "dspy>=2.6.0",
]

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "harbor[cloud]",
    "harbor[tinker]",
    "harbor[computer-1]",
    "harbor[dspy]",
    "harbor[adapter]",
    "harbor-rewardkit[all]",
    "harbor-langsmith",
    "ipykernel>=6.30.1",
    "pytest>=8.4.2",
    "pytest-asyncio>=1.2.0",
    "pytest-cov>=7.0.0",
    "pytest-xdist>=3.8.0",
    "ruff>=0.15.17",
    "ty>=0.0.49",
    "hypothesis>=6.155.0",
]

[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
known-first-party = ["harbor", "harbor_langsmith", "rewardkit"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests", "packages/rewardkit/tests", "packages/harbor-langsmith/tests"]
python_files = ["test_*.py"]
python_classes = ["Test*"]
python_functions = ["test_*"]
addopts = ["-v", "--tb=short", "--strict-markers", "--strict-config"]
markers = [
    "asyncio: marks tests as async tests",
    "runtime: marks tests as runtime tests (may need docker)",
    "unit: marks tests as unit tests (fast, no external dependencies)",
    "integration: marks tests as integration tests (requires external services, could be mocked)",
    "windows_containers: marks tests requiring Docker in Windows container mode",
]
asyncio_mode = "auto"

[tool.coverage.run]

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What This Is

Harbor Viewer is a React SPA for browsing and inspecting Harbor evaluation jobs, trials, and agent trajectories. It operates in two modes: "jobs" mode (browse evaluation results) and "tasks" mode (browse task definitions). The mode is determined by the backend API config.

## Commands

```bash
# Install dependencies
bun install

# Dev server with hot reload (frontend only, http://localhost:5173)
bun dev

# Full dev with backend API (from repo root)
harbor view ./jobs --dev

# Production build (output: build/client/)
bun run build

# Type checking
bun run typecheck

# Deploy to harbor view (copies build to src/harbor/viewer/static/)
harbor view ./jobs --build
```

There are no tests or linting configured in this package. The parent monorepo uses Ruff for Python; this frontend has no equivalent setup.

## Architecture

**Stack**: React 19, React Router 7 (SPA mode, no SSR), Vite, TypeScript strict, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui (new-york style, Radix primitives).

**Path alias**: `~/*` maps to `./app/*` (configured in tsconfig.json).

### Key Files

- `app/root.tsx` - App shell with providers (QueryClient, ThemeProvider, NuqsAdapter, Toaster)
- `app/routes.ts` - All route definitions
- `app/lib/api.ts` - All backend API calls (fetch-based, base URL from `VITE_API_URL` env var or relative)
- `app/lib/types.ts` - TypeScript types for jobs, trials, tasks, ATIF trajectories, filters, pagination
- `app/lib/hooks.ts` - Custom hooks (keyboard table navigation, debounce)
- `app/lib/highlighter.tsx` - Shiki syntax highlighting setup
- `app/components/ui/` - shadcn/ui component library
- `app/components/trajectory/` - Trajectory/ATIF content renderers

### Data Flow

- **Server state**: TanStack React Query for all API data fetching, caching, and mutations
- **URL state**: `nuqs` for search, filters, column visibility, and selection (persisted in query params)
- **Local state**: React useState for transient UI state

### Routes

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/` | Jobs listing (jobs mode) or redirect to /task-definitions (tasks mode) |
| `/compare` | Multi-job comparison grid |
| `/jobs/:jobName` | Single job detail with task table |
| `/jobs/:jobName/tasks/:source/:agent/:modelProvider/:modelName/:taskName` | Task results within a job |
| `.../trials/:trialName` | Trial trajectory viewer |
| `/task-definitions` | Task definition browser |
| `/task-definitions/:taskName` | Task definition detail |

### Adding shadcn/ui Components

Uses the shadcn CLI with config in `components.json`. Components install to `app/components/ui/`.
AGENTS.md

content.subject: Research core architecture & execution lifecycle
content.description:
Investigate Harbor's core abstractions and how a run flows end-to-end. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/agents/base.py` (BaseAgent abstract class), `agents/factory.py`
- `src/harbor/environments/base.py` (BaseEnvironment), `environments/factory.py`
- `src/harbor/orchestrators/` — how trials are orchestrated (concurrency, retries)
- `src/harbor/trial/` and `src/harbor/job.py` — trial vs job models and lifecycle
- `src/harbor/models/` — Pydantic v2 data models (agent, job, task, trial, metric, verifier)
- The async execution lifecycle: environment.start → agent.setup → agent.run → verifier.verify → environment.stop
- `src/harbor/__init__.py` lazy-import pattern

Deliver: a structured summary (key files with paths, the main classes/interfaces, the end-to-end flow of `harbor run`, concurrency model, and notable design patterns). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research agents & environment backends
content.description:
Survey Harbor's agent implementations and execution environment backends. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/agents/installed/` — the 18+ built-in agents (claude-code, openhands, codex, aider, goose, gemini-cli, etc.): how they subclass BaseAgent, setup/run patterns, env var handling, `--ae`/`--agent-env`
- `src/harbor/agents/installed/base.py` (BaseInstalledAgent) if present, `factory.py`, registration in `models/agent/name.py` (AgentName enum)
- `src/harbor/agents/terminus_2/` and internal Terminus agents
- `src/harbor/environments/` backends: docker, daytona, e2b, modal, runloop, apple_container, gke, openshift, novita — what each provides, how BaseEnvironment is implemented (start/stop/exec/upload), EnvironmentType enum & factory
- SUPPORTS_ATIF and SUPPORTS_WINDOWS flags

Deliver: a structured summary (catalog of agents with one-line each, catalog of environments, how to add a new agent/environment, key shared patterns). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research the CLI & command surface
content.description:
Survey Harbor's command-line interface and user-facing workflows. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/cli/main.py` — entry point, how command groups are registered (app.add_typer), the `harbor`/`hr`/`hb` scripts
- Each command module in `src/harbor/cli/`: run/jobs/datasets/trials/tasks/traces/sweeps/adapters/adapter_wizard/publish/analyze/cache/view, plus `admin/`, `annotator/`, `debug_checker/`, `quality_checker/`, `template-*`
- The primary `harbor run` command — its flags (--dataset, --agent, --model, --n-concurrent, --ae, --agent-import-path combined into --agent per recent commit), how it maps to jobs/trials
- Sweeps (parameter sweeps), traces viewing, analysis backend
- Recently added flags: agent/verifier log-filter flags on `harbor trial start`

Deliver: a structured summary (the command tree, the main user workflows from CLI, key flags, how the CLI maps onto the core models). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research benchmarks, verifier, metrics & registry
content.description:
Survey Harbor's benchmark adapter system, verification, metrics, and dataset registry. Focus on:
- `adapters/` — how the 50+ adapters convert external benchmarks (SWE-Bench, Terminal-Bench, Aider Polyglot, LiveCodeBench, etc.) to Harbor task format; the adapter.py/run_adapter.py/template structure
- The task format: task.toml, instruction.md, environment/, tests/ (test.sh writes reward to /logs/verifier/reward.txt or reward.json), solution/
- `src/harbor/verifier/` — the test verification system; `src/harbor/models/verifier/`
- `src/harbor/metrics/` — metrics collection incl. pass@k (recently added unit tests, commit 496293b0)
- `src/harbor/registry/` and `registry.json` (13.9MB!) + `src/harbor/dataset/` — dataset registry, how datasets are listed/resolved (`harbor datasets list`, `dataset@version` syntax)
- `src/harbor/models/trajectories/` — the ATIF (Agent Trajectory Interchange Format)
- `src/harbor/leaderboard/`

Deliver: a structured summary (how adapters work, the canonical task format, the verifier contract, metrics incl pass@k, how the registry/datasets work, ATIF overview). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research web apps, storage, packages & CI/CD
content.description:
Survey Harbor's web apps, storage, publishing, packages, and CI/CD. Focus on:
- `apps/viewer/` — the results viewer web app (React Router + Vite): what it visualizes, tech stack, how it gets data
- `docs/` — the documentation site (Next.js + Fumadocs)
- `src/harbor/storage/` (Supabase backends), `src/harbor/db/`, `src/harbor/auth/` (OAuth callback server) — note the Supabase/PostgREST pagination rules from AGENTS.md
- `src/harbor/publisher/` — package publishing & registry DB; `src/harbor/upload/` and `src/harbor/download/`
- `packages/` — the uv workspace members (harbor-rewardkit, harbor-langsmith); how `pyproject.toml [tool.uv.workspace]` ties them
- `.github/workflows/` — CI/CD (pytest, ruff, ty, claude-code-review, sync-registry, adapter-review, etc.)
- `examples/training/` and the RL optimization claim — how Harbor generates rollouts for RL
- `rfcs/` and `skills/`

Deliver: a structured summary (viewer app overview, docs, storage/auth model, publisher/registry, workspace packages, CI matrix, RL/training hooks). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

from typing import override
from pathlib import Path

from storage3.exceptions import StorageApiError
from tenacity import (
    retry,
    retry_if_exception_type,
    stop_after_attempt,
    wait_exponential,
)

from harbor.auth.client import create_authenticated_client, reset_client
from harbor.storage import resumable
from harbor.storage.base import BaseStorage

BUCKET = "packages"
PACKAGE_STORAGE_TIMEOUT_SEC = 120
UPLOAD_MAX_ATTEMPTS = resumable.UPLOAD_MAX_ATTEMPTS
RETRYABLE_UPLOAD_EXCEPTIONS = resumable.RETRYABLE_UPLOAD_EXCEPTIONS


class SupabaseStorage(BaseStorage):
    @retry(
        retry=retry_if_exception_type(RETRYABLE_UPLOAD_EXCEPTIONS),
        stop=stop_after_attempt(UPLOAD_MAX_ATTEMPTS),
        wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=0.5, min=0.5, max=4.0),
        before_sleep=lambda _: reset_client(),
        reraise=True,
    )
    @override
    async def upload_file(self, file_path: Path, remote_path: str) -> None:
        if file_path.stat().st_size > resumable.RESUMABLE_UPLOAD_THRESHOLD_BYTES:
            uploaded = await resumable.upload_resumable_file(
                file_path,
                remote_path,
                bucket=BUCKET,
            )
            if not uploaded:
                raise StorageApiError(
                    "The resource already exists",
                    "Conflict",
                    409,
                )
            return

        client = await create_authenticated_client()
        data = file_path.read_bytes()
        await client.storage.from_(BUCKET).upload(remote_path, data)

    @override
    async def download_file(self, remote_path: str, file_path: Path) -> None:
        client = await create_authenticated_client(
            storage_client_timeout=PACKAGE_STORAGE_TIMEOUT_SEC
        )
        data = await client.storage.from_(BUCKET).download(remote_path)
        file_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        file_path.write_bytes(data)

115	def _uncached_input(n_input: int | None, n_cache: int | None) -> int | None:
116	    """Derive uncached input token count from raw input + cache totals.
117	
118	    ``AgentContext.n_input_tokens`` is documented to include cached tokens,
119	    so the "uncached" portion the viewer surfaces is total minus cached.
120	    """
121	    if n_input is None:
122	        return None
123	    if n_cache is None:
124	        return n_input

132	def create_app(
133	    folder: Path,
134	    mode: str = "jobs",
135	    static_dir: Path | None = None,
136	) -> FastAPI:
137	    """Create the FastAPI application with routes configured for the given directory.
138	
139	    Args:
140	        folder: Directory containing job/trial data or task definitions
141	        mode: "jobs" for job viewer, "tasks" for task definition browser
142	        static_dir: Optional directory containing static viewer files (index.html, assets/)
143	    """
144	    app = FastAPI(
145	        title="Harbor Viewer",
146	        description="API for browsing Harbor jobs and trials",
147	        version="0.1.0",
148	    )
149	
150	    # Allow CORS for local development
151	    app.add_middleware(
152	        CORSMiddleware,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
153	        allow_origins=["*"],
154	        allow_credentials=True,
155	        allow_methods=["*"],
156	        allow_headers=["*"],
157	    )
158	
159	    @app.get("/api/health")
160	    def health_check() -> dict[str, str]:
161	        """Health check endpoint."""
162	        return {"status": "ok"}
163	
164	    @app.get("/api/config")
165	    def get_config() -> dict[str, Any]:
166	        """Get viewer configuration."""
167	        return {
168	            "folder": str(folder),
169	            "mode": mode,
170	            "environments": [e.value for e in EnvironmentType],
171	        }
172	
173	    @app.get("/api/pricing", response_model=ModelPricing)
174	    def get_model_pricing(
175	        model: str = Query(
176	            ..., description="Model name, e.g. 'gpt-4' or 'openai/gpt-4'"
177	        ),
178	    ) -> ModelPricing:
179	        """Look up per-token pricing for a model from LiteLLM's pricing table.
180	
181	        Falls back to the bare model name when the provider-prefixed form is
182	        not in the table (e.g. ``openai/gpt-4`` -> ``gpt-4``). Cache read
183	        rate falls back to the input rate when not separately listed.
184	        """
185	        try:
186	            import litellm
187	        except ImportError as e:
188	            raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail="LiteLLM not available") from e
189	
190	        pricing: dict[str, Any] | None = None
191	        for key in (model, model.split("/", 1)[-1]):
192	            entry = litellm.model_cost.get(key)
193	            if entry:
194	                pricing = entry
195	                break
196	
197	        if pricing is None:
198	            raise HTTPException(
199	                status_code=404, detail=f"No pricing entry for model '{model}'"
200	            )
201	
202	        input_rate = pricing.get("input_cost_per_token")
203	        output_rate = pricing.get("output_cost_per_token")
204	        cache_read_rate = pricing.get("cache_read_input_token_cost")
205	        if cache_read_rate is None:
206	            cache_read_rate = input_rate
207	
208	        return ModelPricing(
209	            model_name=model,
210	            input_cost_per_token=input_rate,
211	            cache_read_input_token_cost=cache_read_rate,
212	            output_cost_per_token=output_rate,
213	        )
214	
215	    if mode == "tasks":
216	        _register_task_endpoints(app, folder)
217	    else:
218	        _register_job_endpoints(app, folder)
219	        _register_run_endpoints(app, folder)
220	
221	    _register_auth_endpoints(app)
222	
223	    # Serve static viewer files if provided
224	    if static_dir and static_dir.exists():
225	        assets_dir = static_dir / "assets"
226	        if assets_dir.exists():
227	            app.mount(
228	                "/assets", StaticFiles(directory=assets_dir), name="static_assets"
229	            )
230	
231	        fonts_dir = static_dir / "fonts"
232	        if fonts_dir.exists():
233	            app.mount("/fonts", StaticFiles(directory=fonts_dir), name="static_fonts")
234	
235	        @app.get("/favicon.ico")
236	        def favicon() -> FileResponse:
237	            """Serve favicon."""
238	            return FileResponse(static_dir / "favicon.ico")
239	
240	        @app.get("/{path:path}")
241	        def serve_spa(path: str) -> FileResponse:
242	            """Serve the SPA for all non-API routes."""
243	            return FileResponse(static_dir / "index.html")
244	
245	    return app

248	def _validate_return_to(return_to: str | None, request: Request) -> str | None:
249	    """Allow redirects back to localhost or the same host as the viewer API."""
250	    if not return_to:
251	        return None
252	    parsed = urlparse(return_to)
253	    if parsed.scheme not in ("http", "https") or not parsed.netloc:
254	        return None
255	    request_host = urlparse(str(request.base_url)).hostname
256	    if parsed.hostname in ("localhost", "127.0.0.1") or parsed.hostname == request_host:
257	        return return_to
258	    return None
259

261	def _register_auth_endpoints(app: FastAPI) -> None:
262	    """Register OAuth endpoints so the viewer can sign in without the CLI."""
263	
264	    @app.get("/api/auth/status")
265	    async def auth_status() -> dict[str, Any]:
266	        from harbor.auth.handler import get_auth_handler
267	
268	        handler = await get_auth_handler()
269	        if not await handler.is_authenticated():
270	            return {"authenticated": False, "username": None}
271	        return {
272	            "authenticated": True,
273	            "username": await handler.get_github_username(),
274	        }
275	
276	    @app.get("/api/auth/login-url")
277	    async def auth_login_url(
278	        request: Request,
279	        return_to: str | None = Query(
280	            default=None,
281	            description="Frontend URL to redirect to after sign-in completes.",
282	        ),
283	    ) -> dict[str, str]:
284	        from harbor.auth.handler import get_auth_handler
285	
286	        validated_return = _validate_return_to(return_to, request)
287	        callback = str(request.base_url).rstrip("/") + "/auth/callback"
288	        if validated_return:
289	            callback += "?" + urlencode({"return_to": validated_return})
290	
291	        handler = await get_auth_handler()
292	        url = await handler.get_oauth_url(callback)
293	        return {"url": url}
294	
295	    @app.get("/auth/callback", response_model=None)
296	    async def auth_callback(
297	        request: Request,
298	        code: str | None = Query(default=None),
299	        error: str | None = Query(default=None),
300	        return_to: str | None = Query(default=None),
301	    ) -> HTMLResponse | RedirectResponse:
302	        from harbor.auth.callback_server import ERROR_HTML, SUCCESS_HTML
303	        from harbor.auth.errors import AuthenticationError
304	        from harbor.auth.handler import get_auth_handler
305	
306	        if error:
307	            return HTMLResponse(
308	                content=ERROR_HTML.format(error=html.escape(error)),
309	                status_code=400,
310	            )
311	        if not code:
312	            return HTMLResponse(
313	                content=ERROR_HTML.format(
314	                    error=html.escape("No authorization code received")
315	                ),
316	                status_code=400,
317	            )
318	
319	        handler = await get_auth_handler()
320	        try:
321	            await handler.exchange_auth_code(code)
322	        except AuthenticationError as exc:
323	            return HTMLResponse(
324	                content=ERROR_HTML.format(error=html.escape(str(exc))),
325	                status_code=400,
326	            )
327	
328	        validated_return = _validate_return_to(return_to, request)
329	        if validated_return:
330	            return RedirectResponse(validated_return, status_code=302)
331	        return HTMLResponse(content=SUCCESS_HTML, status_code=200)
332	
333	    @app.post("/api/auth/logout")
334	    async def auth_logout() -> dict[str, str]:
335	        from harbor.auth.client import reset_client
336	        from harbor.auth.handler import get_auth_handler, reset_auth_handler
337	
338	        handler = await get_auth_handler()
339	        await handler.logout()
340	        reset_auth_handler()
341	        reset_client()
342	        return {"status": "ok"}

398	    @app.get("/api/task-definitions/filters", response_model=TaskDefinitionFilters)
399	    def get_task_definition_filters() -> TaskDefinitionFilters:
400	        """Get available filter options for task definitions."""
401	        summaries = _get_all_task_definition_summaries()
402	
403	        difficulty_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
404	        category_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
405	        tag_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
406	
407	        for s in summaries:
408	            meta = s.metadata
409	            if "difficulty" in meta and isinstance(meta["difficulty"], str):
410	                difficulty_counts[meta["difficulty"]] += 1
411	            if "category" in meta and isinstance(meta["category"], str):
412	                category_counts[meta["category"]] += 1
413	            if "tags" in meta and isinstance(meta["tags"], list):
414	                for tag in meta["tags"]:
415	                    if isinstance(tag, str):
416	                        tag_counts[tag] += 1
417	
418	        return TaskDefinitionFilters(
419	            difficulties=[
420	                FilterOption(value=v, count=c)
421	                for v, c in sorted(difficulty_counts.items())
422	            ],
423	            categories=[
424	                FilterOption(value=v, count=c)
425	                for v, c in sorted(category_counts.items())
426	            ],
427	            tags=[
428	                FilterOption(value=v, count=c) for v, c in sorted(tag_counts.items())
429	            ],
430	        )

432	    @app.get(
433	        "/api/task-definitions",
434	        response_model=PaginatedResponse[TaskDefinitionSummary],
435	    )
436	    def list_task_definitions(
437	        page: int = Query(default=1, ge=1, description="Page number (1-indexed)"),
438	        page_size: int = Query(
439	            default=100, ge=1, le=100, description="Number of items per page"
440	        ),
441	        q: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Search query"),
442	        difficulty: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by difficulty"),
443	        category: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by category"),
444	        tag: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by tags"),
445	    ) -> PaginatedResponse[TaskDefinitionSummary]:
446	        """List all task definitions with summary information."""
447	        summaries = _get_all_task_definition_summaries()
448	
449	        # Search filter
450	        if q:
451	            query = q.lower()
452	            summaries = [
453	                s
454	                for s in summaries
455	                if query in s.name.lower()
456	                or (s.source and query in s.source.lower())
457	                or any(query in str(v).lower() for v in s.metadata.values())
458	            ]
459	
460	        # Difficulty filter
461	        if difficulty:
462	            summaries = [
463	                s for s in summaries if s.metadata.get("difficulty") in difficulty
464	            ]
465	
466	        # Category filter
467	        if category:
468	            summaries = [s for s in summaries if s.metadata.get("category") in category]
469	
470	        # Tag filter
471	        if tag:
472	            summaries = [
473	                s
474	                for s in summaries
475	                if any(t in s.metadata.get("tags", []) for t in tag)
476	            ]
477	
478	        # Paginate
479	        total = len(summaries)
480	        total_pages = math.ceil(total / page_size) if total > 0 else 0
481	        start_idx = (page - 1) * page_size
482	        end_idx = start_idx + page_size
483	        page_summaries = summaries[start_idx:end_idx]
484	
485	        return PaginatedResponse(
486	            items=page_summaries,
487	            total=total,
488	            page=page,
489	            page_size=page_size,
490	            total_pages=total_pages,
491	        )

524	
525	        raw_files = task_scanner.list_files(name)
526	        return [
527	            FileInfo(
528	                path=cast(str, f["path"]),
529	                name=cast(str, f["name"]),

536	    @app.get(
537	        "/api/task-definitions/{name}/files/{file_path:path}",
538	        response_model=None,
539	    )
540	    def get_task_definition_file(
541	        name: str, file_path: str
542	    ) -> PlainTextResponse | FileResponse:
543	        """Get content of a file in a task definition directory."""
544	        task_dir = _validate_task_name(name)
545	        if not task_dir.exists():
546	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Task '{name}' not found")
547	
548	        # Resolve the path and ensure it's within the task directory
549	        try:
550	            full_path = (task_dir / file_path).resolve()
551	            if (
552	                task_dir.resolve() not in full_path.parents
553	                and full_path != task_dir.resolve()
554	            ):
555	                raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Access denied")
556	        except HTTPException:
557	            raise
558	        except Exception:
559	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid file path")
560	
561	        if not full_path.exists():
562	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")
563	
564	        if full_path.is_dir():
565	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Cannot read directory")
566	
567	        def _format_size(size_bytes: int) -> str:
568	            if size_bytes < 1024:
569	                return f"{size_bytes} bytes"
570	            elif size_bytes < 1024 * 1024:
571	                return f"{size_bytes / 1024:.1f} KB"
572	            else:
573	                return f"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024):.1f} MB"
574	
575	        file_size = full_path.stat().st_size
576	        if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE:
577	            raise HTTPException(
578	                status_code=413,
579	                detail=f"File too large: {_format_size(file_size)} (max {_format_size(MAX_FILE_SIZE)})",
580	            )
581	
582	        image_extensions = {
583	            ".png": "image/png",
584	            ".jpg": "image/jpeg",
585	            ".jpeg": "image/jpeg",
586	            ".gif": "image/gif",
587	            ".webp": "image/webp",
588	        }
589	        suffix = full_path.suffix.lower()
590	        if suffix in image_extensions:
591	            return FileResponse(
592	                path=full_path,
593	                media_type=image_extensions[suffix],
594	                filename=full_path.name,
595	            )
596	
597	        try:
598	            content = full_path.read_text()
599	            return PlainTextResponse(content)
600	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
601	            raise HTTPException(
602	                status_code=415, detail="File is binary and cannot be displayed"
603	            )

606	class _LaunchedRun:
607	    """A `harbor run` subprocess spawned by the launcher."""
608	
609	    def __init__(
610	        self,
611	        process: asyncio.subprocess.Process,
612	        log_path: Path,
613	        work_dir: Path,
614	    ) -> None:
615	        self.process = process
616	        self.log_path = log_path
617	        self.work_dir = work_dir
618	
619	
620	# Launcher-spawned runs, keyed by job name. Lives for the server process.
621	_LAUNCHED_RUNS: dict[str, _LaunchedRun] = {}
622	
623	
624	def _normalize_local_paths(data: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
625	    """Route a local ``datasets[*].path`` that is a single task dir into ``tasks``.
626	
627	    Mirrors the CLI: a path is a task when it is a valid task directory and a
628	    dataset (a directory of tasks) otherwise.
629	    """
630	    from harbor.models.task.task import Task as TaskModel
631	
632	    datasets = data.get("datasets")
633	    if not isinstance(datasets, list):
634	        return data
635	
636	    remaining: list[Any] = []
637	    tasks: list[Any] = list(data.get("tasks") or [])
638	    for ds in datasets:
639	        path = ds.get("path") if isinstance(ds, dict) else None
640	        is_bare_path = bool(
641	            path and not ds.get("name") and not ds.get("repo")  # type: ignore[union-attr]
642	        )
643	        if is_bare_path and TaskModel.is_valid_dir(Path(path)):
644	            tasks.append({"path": path})
645	        else:
646	            remaining.append(ds)
647	
648	    data["datasets"] = remaining
649	    if tasks:
650	        data["tasks"] = tasks
651	    return data
652

654	def _register_run_endpoints(app: FastAPI, jobs_dir: Path) -> None:
655	    """Register endpoints that power the in-viewer ``harbor run`` launcher."""
656	
657	    @app.get("/api/run/options")
658	    def get_run_options() -> dict[str, Any]:
659	        """Available choices and default values for the run launcher form."""
660	        return {
661	            "agents": sorted(AgentName.values()),
662	            "environments": [e.value for e in EnvironmentType],
663	            "resource_modes": [m.value for m in ResourceMode],
664	            "defaults": JobConfig().model_dump(mode="json"),
665	            "jobs_dir": str(jobs_dir),
666	        }
667	
668	    @app.post("/api/run")
669	    async def launch_run(request: Request) -> dict[str, str]:
670	        """Validate a JobConfig and launch it as a detached ``harbor run``."""
671	        data = await request.json()
672	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
673	            raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="Body must be a JSON object.")
674	
675	        data = _normalize_local_paths(data)
676	        data["jobs_dir"] = str(jobs_dir.resolve())
677	
678	        try:
679	            config = JobConfig.model_validate(data)
680	        except Exception as e:
681	            raise HTTPException(
682	                status_code=422, detail=f"Invalid run configuration: {e}"
683	            ) from e
684	
685	        if not config.datasets and not config.tasks:
686	            raise HTTPException(
687	                status_code=422,
688	                detail="Specify a dataset, a task, or a local path to run.",
689	            )
690	
691	        job_name = config.job_name
692	        data["job_name"] = job_name
693	        if (jobs_dir / job_name).exists() or job_name in _LAUNCHED_RUNS:
694	            raise HTTPException(
695	                status_code=409, detail=f"A job named '{job_name}' already exists."
696	            )
697	
698	        # Write the literal (not re-serialized) config so user-entered secrets
699	        # survive: the model's env serializer would redact/templatize them.
700	        work_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="harbor-launch-"))
701	        config_path = work_dir / "config.json"
702	        config_path.write_text(json.dumps(data))
703	        log_path = work_dir / "launch.log"
704	
705	        log_file = log_path.open("w")
706	        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
707	            sys.executable,
708	            "-c",
709	            "from harbor.cli.main import app; app()",
710	            "run",
711	            "-c",
712	            str(config_path),
713	            "-y",
714	            "--quiet",
715	            stdout=log_file,
716	            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.STDOUT,
717	        )
718	        _LAUNCHED_RUNS[job_name] = _LaunchedRun(process, log_path, work_dir)
719	        return {"job_name": job_name}
720	
721	    @app.get("/api/run/{job_name}/status")
722	    def get_run_status(job_name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
723	        """Report launch progress so the UI can wait, then open the job page."""
724	        job_ready = (jobs_dir / job_name / "result.json").exists()
725	        run = _LAUNCHED_RUNS.get(job_name)
726	        if run is None:
727	            return {
728	                "running": False,
729	                "returncode": None,
730	                "job_ready": job_ready,
731	                "log_tail": "",
732	            }
733	
734	        returncode = run.process.returncode
735	        log_tail = ""
736	        if run.log_path.exists():
737	            log_tail = "\n".join(run.log_path.read_text().splitlines()[-40:])
738	        return {
739	            "running": returncode is None,
740	            "returncode": returncode,
741	            "job_ready": job_ready,
742	            "log_tail": log_tail,
743	        }
744	
745	    @app.delete("/api/run/{job_name}")
746	    def stop_run(job_name: str) -> dict[str, bool]:
747	        """Stop a launcher-spawned run. SIGTERM lets harbor clean up environments."""
748	        run = _LAUNCHED_RUNS.get(job_name)
749	        if run is None or run.process.returncode is not None:
750	            raise HTTPException(
751	                status_code=404, detail="No running launch for this job."
752	            )
753	        run.process.terminate()
754	        return {"stopped": True}
755

757	def _register_job_endpoints(app: FastAPI, jobs_dir: Path) -> None:
758	    """Register API endpoints for job browsing."""
759	
760	    scanner = JobScanner(jobs_dir)
761	    resolved_jobs_dir = jobs_dir.resolve()
762	
763	    def _validate_job_path(job_name: str) -> Path:
764	        """Validate job name and return the resolved job directory."""
765	        job_dir = (jobs_dir / job_name).resolve()
766	        if resolved_jobs_dir not in job_dir.parents:
767	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid job name")
768	        return job_dir
769	
770	    def _validate_trial_path(job_name: str, trial_name: str) -> Path:
771	        """Validate trial path and return the resolved trial directory."""
772	        job_dir = _validate_job_path(job_name)
773	        trial_dir = (job_dir / trial_name).resolve()
774	        if job_dir not in trial_dir.parents:
775	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid trial name")
776	        return trial_dir
777	
778	    def _resolve_step_root(trial_dir: Path, step: str | None) -> Path:
779	        """Return the directory to read step-scoped files from.
780	
781	        When ``step`` is set, resolves to ``trial_dir/steps/{step}`` after
782	        validating the path stays inside the trial directory. When it's
783	        ``None``, returns ``trial_dir`` unchanged.
784	        """
785	        if step is None:
786	            return trial_dir
787	        trial_resolved = trial_dir.resolve()
788	        step_dir = (trial_dir / "steps" / step).resolve()
789	        if trial_resolved not in step_dir.parents:
790	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid step name")
791	        if not step_dir.exists():
792	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Step '{step}' not found")
793	        return step_dir
794	
795	    def _get_all_job_summaries() -> list[JobSummary]:
796	        """Get all job summaries (used by both list_jobs and get_job_filters)."""
797	        job_names = scanner.list_jobs()
798	        summaries = []
799	
800	        for name in job_names:
801	            result = scanner.get_job_result(name)
802	            config = scanner.get_job_config(name)
803	
804	            # Extract unique agents, providers, models, datasets, and environment type from config
805	            agents: list[str] = []
806	            providers: list[str] = []
807	            models: list[str] = []
808	            datasets: list[str] = []
809	            environment_type: str | None = None
810	            if config:
811	                agents = sorted(
812	                    set(agent.name for agent in config.agents if agent.name is not None)
813	                )
814	                # Extract dataset names
815	                for ds in config.datasets:
816	                    if ds.is_local():
817	                        assert ds.path is not None
818	                        datasets.append(ds.path.name)
819	                    elif ds.is_package() or ds.is_registry():
820	                        assert ds.name is not None
821	                        datasets.append(ds.name)
822	                datasets = sorted(set(datasets))
823	                # Extract provider from model_name (format: "provider/model")
824	                for agent in config.agents:
825	                    if agent.model_name:
826	                        parts = agent.model_name.split("/", 1)
827	                        if len(parts) == 2:
828	                            providers.append(parts[0])
829	                            models.append(parts[1])
830	                        else:
831	                            models.append(agent.model_name)
832	                providers = sorted(set(providers))
833	                models = sorted(set(models))
834	                if config.environment.type:
835	                    environment_type = config.environment.type.value
836	
837	            if result:
838	                # Extract evals from stats
839	                evals = {
840	                    key: EvalSummary(metrics=eval_stats.metrics)
841	                    for key, eval_stats in result.stats.evals.items()
842	                    if eval_stats.metrics
843	                }
844	                summaries.append(
845	                    JobSummary(
846	                        name=name,
847	                        id=result.id,
848	                        started_at=result.started_at,
849	                        updated_at=result.updated_at,
850	                        finished_at=result.finished_at,
851	                        n_total_trials=result.n_total_trials,
852	                        n_completed_trials=result.stats.n_completed_trials,
853	                        n_errored_trials=result.stats.n_errored_trials,
854	                        datasets=datasets,
855	                        agents=agents,
856	                        providers=providers,
857	                        models=models,
858	                        environment_type=environment_type,
859	                        evals=evals,
860	                        total_input_tokens=_uncached_input(
861	                            result.stats.n_input_tokens, result.stats.n_cache_tokens
862	                        ),
863	                        total_cached_input_tokens=result.stats.n_cache_tokens,
864	                        total_output_tokens=result.stats.n_output_tokens,
865	                        total_cost_usd=result.stats.cost_usd,
866	                    )
867	                )
868	            else:
869	                summaries.append(
870	                    JobSummary(
871	                        name=name,
872	                        datasets=datasets,
873	                        agents=agents,
874	                        providers=providers,
875	                        models=models,
876	                        environment_type=environment_type,
877	                    )
878	                )
879	
880	        # Sort by started_at descending (most recent first), jobs without started_at go last
881	        summaries.sort(
882	            key=lambda s: (s.started_at is not None, s.started_at),
883	            reverse=True,
884	        )
885	        return summaries

889	        """Get available filter options for jobs list."""

891	
892	        summaries = _get_all_job_summaries()
893	
894	        # Count occurrences of agents, providers, and models
895	        agent_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
896	        provider_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
897	        model_counts: Counter[str] = Counter()
898	
899	        for summary in summaries:
900	            for agent in summary.agents:
901	                agent_counts[agent] += 1
902	            for provider in summary.providers:
903	                provider_counts[provider] += 1

905	                model_counts[model] += 1
906	
907	        return JobFilters(
908	            agents=[
909	                FilterOption(value=v, count=c) for v, c in sorted(agent_counts.items())
910	            ],
911	            providers=[
912	                FilterOption(value=v, count=c)
913	                for v, c in sorted(provider_counts.items())
914	            ],
915	            models=[
916	                FilterOption(value=v, count=c) for v, c in sorted(model_counts.items())
917	            ],
918	        )

920	    @app.get("/api/jobs", response_model=PaginatedResponse[JobSummary])
921	    def list_jobs(
922	        page: int = Query(default=1, ge=1, description="Page number (1-indexed)"),
923	        page_size: int = Query(
924	            default=100, ge=1, le=100, description="Number of items per page"
925	        ),
926	        q: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Search query"),
927	        agent: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by agent names"),
928	        provider: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by provider names"),
929	        model: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by model names"),
930	        date: list[str] = Query(
931	            default=[],
932	            description="Filter by date ranges (today, week, month)",
933	        ),
934	    ) -> PaginatedResponse[JobSummary]:
935	        """List all jobs with summary information."""
936	        from datetime import datetime, timedelta
937	
938	        summaries = _get_all_job_summaries()
939	
940	        # Filter by search query
941	        if q:
942	            query = q.lower()
943	            summaries = [
944	                s
945	                for s in summaries
946	                if query in s.name.lower()
947	                or any(query in agent_name.lower() for agent_name in s.agents)
948	                or any(query in provider_name.lower() for provider_name in s.providers)
949	                or any(query in model_name.lower() for model_name in s.models)
950	            ]
951	
952	        # Filter by agents (OR within agents)
953	        if agent:
954	            summaries = [s for s in summaries if any(a in s.agents for a in agent)]
955	
956	        # Filter by providers (OR within providers)
957	        if provider:
958	            summaries = [
959	                s for s in summaries if any(p in s.providers for p in provider)
960	            ]
961	
962	        # Filter by models (OR within models)
963	        if model:
964	            summaries = [s for s in summaries if any(m in s.models for m in model)]
965	
966	        # Filter by date (OR within dates - use the most permissive)
967	        if date:
968	            now = datetime.now()
969	            cutoffs = []
970	            for d in date:
971	                if d == "today":
972	                    cutoffs.append(now - timedelta(days=1))
973	                elif d == "week":
974	                    cutoffs.append(now - timedelta(weeks=1))
975	                elif d == "month":
976	                    cutoffs.append(now - timedelta(days=30))
977	
978	            if cutoffs:
979	                # Use the earliest cutoff (most permissive)
980	                cutoff = min(cutoffs)
981	                summaries = [
982	                    s
983	                    for s in summaries
984	                    if s.started_at is not None
985	                    and s.started_at.replace(tzinfo=None) >= cutoff
986	                ]
987	
988	        # Paginate
989	        total = len(summaries)
990	        total_pages = math.ceil(total / page_size) if total > 0 else 0
991	        start_idx = (page - 1) * page_size
992	        end_idx = start_idx + page_size
993	        page_summaries = summaries[start_idx:end_idx]
994	
995	        return PaginatedResponse(
996	            items=page_summaries,
997	            total=total,
998	            page=page,
999	            page_size=page_size,
1000	            total_pages=total_pages,
1001	        )

1078	    @app.get("/api/jobs/{job_name}/upload")
1079	    async def get_upload_status(job_name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
1080	        """Probe whether this job is already on Harbor Hub.
1081	
1082	        Returns one of:
1083	          * ``uploaded`` — job row exists server-side (accessible to the caller).
1084	          * ``in_progress`` — local job has not written ``result.json`` yet.
1085	          * ``not_uploaded`` — no row yet (or RLS hides it from the caller).
1086	          * ``unauthenticated`` — sign in via the viewer or run ``harbor auth login``.
1087	          * ``unavailable`` — network / RPC error reaching Harbor Hub.
1088	          * ``unknown`` — unexpected error; conservative fallback.
1089	        """
1090	        from harbor.constants import HARBOR_VIEWER_JOBS_URL
1091	        from harbor.models.job.result import JobResult
1092	        from harbor.upload.db_client import UploadDB
1093	
1094	        job_dir = _validate_job_path(job_name)
1095	        if not job_dir.exists():
1096	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job '{job_name}' not found")
1097	
1098	        result_path = job_dir / "result.json"
1099	        if not result_path.exists():
1100	            # Run still in progress / never completed → nothing to probe.
1101	            return {"status": "in_progress", "job_id": None, "view_url": None}
1102	
1103	        try:
1104	            job_result = JobResult.model_validate_json(result_path.read_text())
1105	        except Exception:
1106	            return {"status": "unknown", "job_id": None, "view_url": None}
1107	
1108	        job_id = str(job_result.id)
1109	        db = UploadDB()
1110	        try:
1111	            await db.get_user_id()
1112	        except RuntimeError as exc:
1113	            if "Not authenticated" in str(exc):
1114	                return {"status": "unauthenticated", "job_id": job_id, "view_url": None}
1115	            return {"status": "unavailable", "job_id": job_id, "view_url": None}
1116	        except Exception:
1117	            return {"status": "unavailable", "job_id": job_id, "view_url": None}
1118	
1119	        try:
1120	            visibility = await db.get_job_visibility(job_result.id)
1121	        except Exception:
1122	            return {"status": "unavailable", "job_id": job_id, "view_url": None}
1123	
1124	        if visibility is None:
1125	            return {"status": "not_uploaded", "job_id": job_id, "view_url": None}
1126	        return {
1127	            "status": "uploaded",
1128	            "job_id": job_id,
1129	            "view_url": f"{HARBOR_VIEWER_JOBS_URL}/{job_id}",
1130	        }
1131	
1132	    @app.post("/api/jobs/{job_name}/upload")
1133	    async def upload_job(
1134	        job_name: str, request: UploadJobRequest | None = None
1135	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1136	        """Upload a job to Harbor Hub.
1137	
1138	        ``visibility`` (from the request body) follows the same tri-state
1139	        rules as the CLI's ``--public`` / ``--private`` flag: ``None`` means
1140	        "private for new jobs, unchanged for re-uploads"; ``"public"`` /
1141	        ``"private"`` always apply. The modal in the viewer's upload button
1142	        surfaces the public/private choice.
1143	        """
1144	        from harbor.constants import HARBOR_VIEWER_JOBS_URL
1145	        from harbor.upload.uploader import Uploader
1146	
1147	        job_dir = _validate_job_path(job_name)
1148	        if not job_dir.exists():
1149	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job '{job_name}' not found")
1150	
1151	        if (
1152	            not (job_dir / "result.json").exists()
1153	            or not (job_dir / "config.json").exists()
1154	        ):
1155	            raise HTTPException(
1156	                status_code=400,
1157	                detail=(
1158	                    f"Job '{job_name}' is missing result.json / config.json — "
1159	                    "it may still be running or the run was interrupted."
1160	                ),
1161	            )
1162	
1163	        visibility = request.visibility if request is not None else None
1164	        upload_visibility: PublicJobVisibility | None
1165	        if visibility == "public":
1166	            upload_visibility = "public"
1167	        elif visibility == "private":
1168	            upload_visibility = "private"
1169	        elif visibility is None:
1170	            upload_visibility = None
1171	        else:
1172	            raise HTTPException(
1173	                status_code=400,
1174	                detail=(
1175	                    f"Invalid visibility {visibility!r}; must be "
1176	                    "'public', 'private', or omitted."
1177	                ),
1178	            )
1179	
1180	        uploader = Uploader()
1181	        try:
1182	            result = await uploader.upload_job(
1183	                job_dir,
1184	                visibility=upload_visibility,
1185	            )
1186	        except RuntimeError as exc:
1187	            # Hot-path: surface the auth prompt inline so the UI can route
1188	            # the user to sign-in rather than just showing the raw error.
1189	            if "Not authenticated" in str(exc):
1190	                raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail=str(exc)) from None
1191	            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(exc)) from None
1192	
1193	        return {
1194	            "job_id": result.job_id,
1195	            "view_url": f"{HARBOR_VIEWER_JOBS_URL}/{result.job_id}",
1196	            "n_trials_uploaded": result.n_trials_uploaded,
1197	            "n_trials_skipped": result.n_trials_skipped,
1198	            "n_trials_failed": result.n_trials_failed,
1199	            "total_time_sec": result.total_time_sec,
1200	            "errors": [
1201	                {"trial_name": r.trial_name, "error": r.error}
1202	                for r in result.trial_results
1203	                if r.error is not None
1204	            ],
1205	        }

1222	    @app.get("/api/compare", response_model=ComparisonGridData)
1223	    def get_comparison_data(
1224	        job: list[str] = Query(..., description="Job names to compare"),
1225	    ) -> ComparisonGridData:
1226	        """Get comparison grid data for multiple jobs."""
1227	        # Validate all jobs exist
1228	        existing_jobs = scanner.list_jobs()
1229	        for job_name in job:
1230	            if job_name not in existing_jobs:
1231	                raise HTTPException(
1232	                    status_code=404, detail=f"Job '{job_name}' not found"
1233	                )
1234	
1235	        # Collect all task summaries from all jobs
1236	        # Group by (source, task_name) for tasks and (job_name, agent_name, model_provider, model_name) for agent_models
1237	        tasks_set: set[tuple[str | None, str]] = set()
1238	        agent_models_set: set[tuple[str, str | None, str | None, str | None]] = set()
1239	        # cells[task_key][am_key] = ComparisonCell
1240	        cells: dict[str, dict[str, ComparisonCell]] = {}
1241	
1242	        for job_name in job:
1243	            summaries = _get_all_task_summaries(job_name)
1244	            for summary in summaries:
1245	                task_key = f"{summary.source or ''}::{summary.task_name}"
1246	                am_key = f"{job_name}::{summary.agent_name or ''}::{summary.model_provider or ''}::{summary.model_name or ''}"
1247	
1248	                tasks_set.add((summary.source, summary.task_name))
1249	                agent_models_set.add(
1250	                    (
1251	                        job_name,
1252	                        summary.agent_name,
1253	                        summary.model_provider,
1254	                        summary.model_name,
1255	                    )
1256	                )
1257	
1258	                if task_key not in cells:
1259	                    cells[task_key] = {}
1260	
1261	                cells[task_key][am_key] = ComparisonCell(
1262	                    job_name=job_name,
1263	                    avg_reward=summary.avg_reward,
1264	                    avg_duration_ms=summary.avg_duration_ms,
1265	                    n_trials=summary.n_trials,
1266	                    n_completed=summary.n_completed,
1267	                )
1268	
1269	        # Calculate average reward per task (across all agent_models)
1270	        task_avg_rewards: dict[str, float] = {}
1271	        for source, task_name in tasks_set:
1272	            task_key = f"{source or ''}::{task_name}"
1273	            task_cells = cells.get(task_key, {})
1274	            if task_cells:
1275	                rewards = [cell.avg_reward or 0.0 for cell in task_cells.values()]
1276	                task_avg_rewards[task_key] = sum(rewards) / len(rewards)
1277	            else:
1278	                task_avg_rewards[task_key] = 0.0
1279	
1280	        # Build task list sorted by average reward (high to low), then alphabetically
1281	        tasks = sorted(
1282	            [
1283	                ComparisonTask(
1284	                    source=source,
1285	                    task_name=task_name,
1286	                    key=f"{source or ''}::{task_name}",
1287	                )
1288	                for source, task_name in tasks_set
1289	            ],
1290	            key=lambda t: (
1291	                -task_avg_rewards.get(t.key, 0.0),
1292	                t.source or "",
1293	                t.task_name,
1294	            ),
1295	        )
1296	
1297	        # Calculate average reward per agent_model (across all tasks)
1298	        am_avg_rewards: dict[str, float] = {}
1299	        for job_name, agent_name, model_provider, model_name in agent_models_set:
1300	            am_key = f"{job_name}::{agent_name or ''}::{model_provider or ''}::{model_name or ''}"
1301	            rewards = []
1302	            for task_key, task_cells in cells.items():
1303	                if am_key in task_cells:
1304	                    rewards.append(task_cells[am_key].avg_reward or 0.0)
1305	            am_avg_rewards[am_key] = sum(rewards) / len(rewards) if rewards else 0.0
1306	
1307	        # Build agent_model list sorted by average reward (high to low), then alphabetically
1308	        agent_models = sorted(
1309	            [
1310	                ComparisonAgentModel(
1311	                    job_name=job_name,
1312	                    agent_name=agent_name,
1313	                    model_provider=model_provider,
1314	                    model_name=model_name,
1315	                    key=f"{job_name}::{agent_name or ''}::{model_provider or ''}::{model_name or ''}",
1316	                )
1317	                for job_name, agent_name, model_provider, model_name in agent_models_set
1318	            ],
1319	            key=lambda am: (
1320	                -am_avg_rewards.get(am.key, 0.0),
1321	                am.job_name,
1322	                am.agent_name or "",
1323	                am.model_provider or "",
1324	                am.model_name or "",
1325	            ),
1326	        )
1327	
1328	        return ComparisonGridData(
1329	            tasks=tasks,
1330	            agent_models=agent_models,
1331	            cells=cells,
1332	        )
1333

1344	    def _get_all_task_summaries(job_name: str) -> list[TaskSummary]:
1345	        """Get all task summaries for a job (used by list_tasks and get_task_filters)."""
1346	        trial_names = scanner.list_trials(job_name)
1347	        if not trial_names:
1348	            return []
1349	
1350	        # Group trials by (agent_name, model_provider, model_name, source, task_name)
1351	        groups: dict[
1352	            tuple[str | None, str | None, str | None, str | None, str],
1353	            TaskGroupStats,
1354	        ] = {}
1355	
1356	        for name in trial_names:
1357	            result = scanner.get_trial_result(job_name, name)
1358	            if not result:
1359	                config = scanner.get_trial_config(job_name, name)
1360	                if not config:
1361	                    continue
1362	                agent_name = agent_name_from_config(config)

1399	            source = result.source

1401	
1402	            key = (

1410	            if key not in groups:

1578	    @app.get(
1579	        "/api/jobs/{job_name}/tasks", response_model=PaginatedResponse[TaskSummary]
1580	    )
1581	    def list_tasks(
1582	        job_name: str,
1583	        page: int = Query(default=1, ge=1, description="Page number (1-indexed)"),
1584	        page_size: int = Query(
1585	            default=100, ge=1, le=100, description="Number of items per page"
1586	        ),
1587	        q: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Search query"),
1588	        agent: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by agent names"),
1589	        provider: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by provider names"),
1590	        model: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by model names"),
1591	        task: list[str] = Query(default=[], description="Filter by task names"),
1592	        sort_by: str | None = Query(
1593	            default=None,
1594	            description="Field to sort by (task_name, agent_name, model_provider, model_name, source, n_trials, n_errors, avg_duration_ms, avg_reward, avg_input_tokens, avg_cached_input_tokens, avg_output_tokens, avg_cost_usd)",
1595	        ),
1596	        sort_order: str = Query(default="asc", description="Sort order (asc or desc)"),
1597	    ) -> PaginatedResponse[TaskSummary]:
1598	        """List tasks in a job, grouped by agent + model + source + task_name."""
1599	        if job_name not in scanner.list_jobs():
1600	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job '{job_name}' not found")
1601	
1602	        summaries = _get_all_task_summaries(job_name)
1603	
1604	        # Filter by search query (searches task, agent, provider, model, dataset)
1605	        if q:
1606	            query = q.lower()
1607	            summaries = [
1608	                s
1609	                for s in summaries
1610	                if query in s.task_name.lower()
1611	                or (s.agent_name and query in s.agent_name.lower())
1612	                or (s.model_provider and query in s.model_provider.lower())
1613	                or (s.model_name and query in s.model_name.lower())
1614	                or (s.source and query in s.source.lower())
1615	            ]
1616	
1617	        # Filter by agents
1618	        if agent:
1619	            summaries = [s for s in summaries if s.agent_name in agent]
1620	
1621	        # Filter by providers
1622	        if provider:
1623	            summaries = [s for s in summaries if s.model_provider in provider]
1624	
1625	        # Filter by models
1626	        if model:
1627	            summaries = [s for s in summaries if s.model_name in model]
1628	
1629	        # Filter by task names
1630	        if task:
1631	            summaries = [s for s in summaries if s.task_name in task]
1632	
1633	        # Sort
1634	        if sort_by:
1635	            reverse = sort_order == "desc"
1636	            if sort_by == "task_name":
1637	                summaries.sort(key=lambda s: s.task_name or "", reverse=reverse)
1638	            elif sort_by == "agent_name":
1639	                summaries.sort(key=lambda s: s.agent_name or "", reverse=reverse)
1640	            elif sort_by == "model_provider":
1641	                summaries.sort(key=lambda s: s.model_provider or "", reverse=reverse)
1642	            elif sort_by == "model_name":
1643	                summaries.sort(key=lambda s: s.model_name or "", reverse=reverse)
1644	            elif sort_by == "source":
1645	                summaries.sort(key=lambda s: s.source or "", reverse=reverse)
1646	            elif sort_by == "n_trials":
1647	                summaries.sort(key=lambda s: s.n_trials, reverse=reverse)
1648	            elif sort_by == "n_errors":
1649	                summaries.sort(key=lambda s: s.n_errors, reverse=reverse)
1650	            elif sort_by == "avg_duration_ms":
1651	                # Put None values at the end
1652	                summaries.sort(
1653	                    key=lambda s: (
1654	                        s.avg_duration_ms is None,
1655	                        s.avg_duration_ms or 0,
1656	                    ),
1657	                    reverse=reverse,
1658	                )
1659	            elif sort_by == "avg_reward":
1660	                summaries.sort(
1661	                    key=lambda s: (s.avg_reward is None, s.avg_reward or 0),
1662	                    reverse=reverse,
1663	                )
1664	            elif sort_by == "exception_types":
1665	                summaries.sort(
1666	                    key=lambda s: s.exception_types[0] if s.exception_types else "",
1667	                    reverse=reverse,
1668	                )
1669	            elif sort_by == "avg_input_tokens":
1670	                summaries.sort(
1671	                    key=lambda s: (s.avg_input_tokens is None, s.avg_input_tokens or 0),
1672	                    reverse=reverse,
1673	                )
1674	            elif sort_by == "avg_cached_input_tokens":
1675	                summaries.sort(
1676	                    key=lambda s: (
1677	                        s.avg_cached_input_tokens is None,
1678	                        s.avg_cached_input_tokens or 0,
1679	                    ),
1680	                    reverse=reverse,
1681	                )
1682	            elif sort_by == "avg_output_tokens":
1683	                summaries.sort(
1684	                    key=lambda s: (
1685	                        s.avg_output_tokens is None,
1686	                        s.avg_output_tokens or 0,
1687	                    ),
1688	                    reverse=reverse,
1689	                )
1690	            elif sort_by == "avg_cost_usd":
1691	                summaries.sort(
1692	                    key=lambda s: (s.avg_cost_usd is None, s.avg_cost_usd or 0),
1693	                    reverse=reverse,
1694	                )
1695	
1696	        # Paginate
1697	        total = len(summaries)
1698	        total_pages = math.ceil(total / page_size) if total > 0 else 0
1699	        start_idx = (page - 1) * page_size
1700	        end_idx = start_idx + page_size
1701	        page_summaries = summaries[start_idx:end_idx]
1702	
1703	        return PaginatedResponse(
1704	            items=page_summaries,
1705	            total=total,
1706	            page=page,
1707	            page_size=page_size,
1708	            total_pages=total_pages,
1709	        )

1711	    @app.get(
1712	        "/api/jobs/{job_name}/trials",
1713	        response_model=PaginatedResponse[TrialSummary],
1714	    )
1715	    def list_trials(
1716	        job_name: str,
1717	        task_name: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Filter by task name"),
1718	        source: str | None = Query(
1719	            default=None, description="Filter by source/dataset"
1720	        ),
1721	        agent_name: str | None = Query(
1722	            default=None, description="Filter by agent name"
1723	        ),
1724	        model_name: str | None = Query(
1725	            default=None, description="Filter by model name"
1726	        ),
1727	        page: int = Query(default=1, ge=1, description="Page number (1-indexed)"),
1728	        page_size: int = Query(
1729	            default=100, ge=1, le=100, description="Number of items per page"
1730	        ),
1731	    ) -> PaginatedResponse[TrialSummary]:
1732	        """List trials in a job with pagination and optional filtering."""
1733	        trial_names = scanner.list_trials(job_name)
1734	        if not trial_names:
1735	            if job_name not in scanner.list_jobs():
1736	                raise HTTPException(
1737	                    status_code=404, detail=f"Job '{job_name}' not found"
1738	                )
1739	            return PaginatedResponse(
1740	                items=[], total=0, page=page, page_size=page_size, total_pages=0
1741	            )
1742	
1743	        # Build list of trial summaries with filtering
1744	        all_summaries = []
1745	        for name in trial_names:
1746	            result = scanner.get_trial_result(job_name, name)
1747	            if not result:
1748	                config = scanner.get_trial_config(job_name, name)
1749	                if not config:
1750	                    continue
1751	                summary = trial_summary_from_config(name, config)
1752	                if task_name is not None and summary.task_name != task_name:
1753	                    continue
1754	                if source is not None and summary.source != source:
1755	                    continue
1756	                if agent_name is not None and summary.agent_name != agent_name:
1757	                    continue
1758	                if model_name is not None:
1759	                    full_model = (
1760	                        f"{summary.model_provider}/{summary.model_name}"
1761	                        if summary.model_provider and summary.model_name
1762	                        else summary.model_name
1763	                    )
1764	                    if full_model != model_name:
1765	                        continue
1766	                all_summaries.append(summary)
1767	                continue
1768	
1769	            # Apply filters
1770	            if task_name is not None and result.task_name != task_name:
1771	                continue
1772	            if source is not None and result.source != source:
1773	                continue
1774	            result_agent_name = agent_name_from_result(result)
1775	            if agent_name is not None and result_agent_name != agent_name:
1776	                continue
1777	            model_info = result.agent_info.model_info
1778	            # Build full model name (provider/name) to match frontend format
1779	            if model_info and model_info.provider:
1780	                result_full_model_name = f"{model_info.provider}/{model_info.name}"
1781	            elif model_info:
1782	                result_full_model_name = model_info.name
1783	            else:
1784	                result_full_model_name = None
1785	            if model_name is not None and result_full_model_name != model_name:
1786	                continue
1787	
1788	            # Extract primary reward if available
1789	            reward = None
1790	            if result.verifier_result and result.verifier_result.rewards:
1791	                reward = result.verifier_result.rewards.get("reward")
1792	
1793	            result_model_provider = model_info.provider if model_info else None
1794	            result_model_name = model_info.name if model_info else None
1795	
1796	            n_input, n_cache, n_output, cost = result.compute_token_cost_totals()
1797	
1798	            all_summaries.append(
1799	                TrialSummary(
1800	                    name=name,
1801	                    task_name=result.task_name,
1802	                    id=result.id,
1803	                    source=result.source,
1804	                    agent_name=result_agent_name,
1805	                    model_provider=result_model_provider,
1806	                    model_name=result_model_name,
1807	                    reward=reward,
1808	                    error_type=(
1809	                        result.exception_info.exception_type
1810	                        if result.exception_info
1811	                        else None
1812	                    ),
1813	                    started_at=result.started_at,
1814	                    finished_at=result.finished_at,
1815	                    input_tokens=_uncached_input(n_input, n_cache),
1816	                    cached_input_tokens=n_cache,
1817	                    output_tokens=n_output,
1818	                    cost_usd=cost,
1819	                )
1820	            )
1821	
1822	        # Paginate
1823	        total = len(all_summaries)
1824	        total_pages = math.ceil(total / page_size) if total > 0 else 0
1825	        start_idx = (page - 1) * page_size
1826	        end_idx = start_idx + page_size
1827	        page_summaries = all_summaries[start_idx:end_idx]
1828	
1829	        return PaginatedResponse(
1830	            items=page_summaries,
1831	            total=total,
1832	            page=page,
1833	            page_size=page_size,
1834	            total_pages=total_pages,
1835	        )
