1861	    @app.post("/api/jobs/{job_name}/trials/{trial_name}/summarize")
1862	    async def summarize_trial(
1863	        job_name: str, trial_name: str, request: TrialSummarizeRequest
1864	    ) -> dict[str, str | None]:
1865	        """Generate an analysis for a single trial as a Harbor job (harbor analyze)."""
1866	        trial_dir = _validate_trial_path(job_name, trial_name)
1867	        if not trial_dir.exists():
1868	            raise HTTPException(
1869	                status_code=404,
1870	                detail=f"Trial '{trial_name}' not found in job '{job_name}'",
1871	            )
1872	
1873	        from harbor.analyze.analyzer import run_analyze
1874	
1875	        report, _ = await run_analyze(
1876	            path=trial_dir,
1877	            agent=request.agent,
1878	            model=request.model,
1879	            environment=EnvironmentType(request.environment),
1880	            jobs_dir=jobs_dir,
1881	        )
1882	        result = report.results[0]
1883	        if result.error:
1884	            raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=result.error)
1885	
1886	        return {"summary": result.summary}

1916	        job_name: str,
1917	        trial_name: str,
1918	        step: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Step name to scope to"),
1919	    ) -> dict[str, str | dict[str, Any] | None]:
1920	        """Get verifier output files from the trial's verifier directory.
1921	
1922	        Returns test-stdout.txt, test-stderr.txt, ctrf.json as text, plus reward.json
1923	        and reward-details.json (rewardkit) parsed as JSON.
1924	        """
1925	        trial_dir = _validate_trial_path(job_name, trial_name)
1926	        if not trial_dir.exists():
1927	            raise HTTPException(
1928	                status_code=404,
1929	                detail=f"Trial '{trial_name}' not found in job '{job_name}'",
1930	            )
1931	
1932	        verifier_dir = _resolve_step_root(trial_dir, step) / "verifier"
1933	
1934	        def _read_text(path: Path) -> str | None:
1935	            if not path.exists():
1936	                return None
1937	            try:
1938	                return path.read_text()
1939	            except Exception:
1940	                return "[Error reading file]"
1941	
1942	        def _read_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
1943	            if not path.exists():
1944	                return None
1945	            try:
1946	                parsed = json.loads(path.read_text())
1947	            except Exception:
1948	                return None
1949	            return parsed if isinstance(parsed, dict) else None
1950	
1951	        return {
1952	            "stdout": _read_text(verifier_dir / "test-stdout.txt"),
1953	            "stderr": _read_text(verifier_dir / "test-stderr.txt"),
1954	            "ctrf": _read_text(verifier_dir / "ctrf.json"),
1955	            "reward": _read_json(verifier_dir / "reward.json"),
1956	            "reward_details": _read_json(verifier_dir / "reward-details.json"),
1957	        }
1958	
1959	    @app.get("/api/jobs/{job_name}/trials/{trial_name}/files")
1960	    def list_trial_files(
1961	        job_name: str,
1962	        trial_name: str,
1963	        step: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Step name to scope to"),
1964	    ) -> list[FileInfo]:
1965	        """List all files in a trial directory (optionally a specific step)."""
1966	        trial_dir = _validate_trial_path(job_name, trial_name)
1967	        if not trial_dir.exists():
1968	            raise HTTPException(
1969	                status_code=404,
1970	                detail=f"Trial '{trial_name}' not found in job '{job_name}'",
1971	            )
1972	
1973	        root = _resolve_step_root(trial_dir, step)
1974	        files: list[FileInfo] = []
1975	
1976	        def scan_dir(dir_path: Path, relative_base: str = "") -> None:
1977	            try:
1978	                for item in sorted(dir_path.iterdir()):
1979	                    relative_path = (
1980	                        f"{relative_base}/{item.name}" if relative_base else item.name
1981	                    )
1982	                    if item.is_dir():
1983	                        files.append(
1984	                            FileInfo(
1985	                                path=relative_path,
1986	                                name=item.name,
1987	                                is_dir=True,
1988	                                size=None,
1989	                            )
1990	                        )
1991	                        scan_dir(item, relative_path)
1992	                    else:
1993	                        files.append(
1994	                            FileInfo(
1995	                                path=relative_path,
1996	                                name=item.name,
1997	                                is_dir=False,
1998	                                size=item.stat().st_size,
1999	                            )
2000	                        )
2001	            except PermissionError:
2002	                pass
2003	
2004	        scan_dir(root)
2005	        return files
2006	
2007	    @app.get(
2008	        "/api/jobs/{job_name}/trials/{trial_name}/files/{file_path:path}",
2009	        response_model=None,
2010	    )
2011	    def get_trial_file(
2012	        job_name: str,
2013	        trial_name: str,
2014	        file_path: str,
2015	        step: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Step name to scope to"),
2016	    ) -> PlainTextResponse | FileResponse:
2017	        """Get content of a file in a trial directory.
2018	
2019	        For text files, returns PlainTextResponse with the content.
2020	        For image files (png, jpg, gif, webp), returns FileResponse with appropriate media type.
2021	        """
2022	        trial_dir = _validate_trial_path(job_name, trial_name)
2023	        if not trial_dir.exists():
2024	            raise HTTPException(
2025	                status_code=404,
2026	                detail=f"Trial '{trial_name}' not found in job '{job_name}'",
2027	            )
2028	
2029	        root = _resolve_step_root(trial_dir, step)
2030	
2031	        # Resolve the path and ensure it's within the trial directory (prevent traversal)
2032	        try:
2033	            full_path = (root / file_path).resolve()
2034	            if trial_dir.resolve() not in full_path.parents:
2035	                raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Access denied")
2036	        except HTTPException:
2037	            raise
2038	        except Exception:
2039	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid file path")
2040	
2041	        if not full_path.exists():
2042	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="File not found")
2043	
2044	        if full_path.is_dir():
2045	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Cannot read directory")
2046	
2047	        def _format_size(size_bytes: int) -> str:
2048	            """Format bytes as human-readable string."""
2049	            if size_bytes < 1024:
2050	                return f"{size_bytes} bytes"
2051	            elif size_bytes < 1024 * 1024:
2052	                return f"{size_bytes / 1024:.1f} KB"
2053	            else:
2054	                return f"{size_bytes / (1024 * 1024):.1f} MB"
2055	
2056	        # Check file size
2057	        file_size = full_path.stat().st_size
2058	        if file_size > MAX_FILE_SIZE:
2059	            raise HTTPException(
2060	                status_code=413,
2061	                detail=f"File too large: {_format_size(file_size)} (max {_format_size(MAX_FILE_SIZE)})",
2062	            )
2063	
2064	        # Handle image files - serve as binary with correct media type
2065	        image_extensions = {
2066	            ".png": "image/png",
2067	            ".jpg": "image/jpeg",
2068	            ".jpeg": "image/jpeg",
2069	            ".gif": "image/gif",
2070	            ".webp": "image/webp",
2071	            ".svg": "image/svg+xml",
2072	        }
2073	        suffix = full_path.suffix.lower()
2074	        if suffix in image_extensions:
2075	            return FileResponse(
2076	                path=full_path,
2077	                media_type=image_extensions[suffix],
2078	                filename=full_path.name,
2079	            )
2080	
2081	        # For text files, read and return as plain text
2082	        try:
2083	            content = full_path.read_text()
2084	            return PlainTextResponse(content)
2085	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
2086	            raise HTTPException(
2087	                status_code=415, detail="File is binary and cannot be displayed"
2088	            )
2089	
2090	    @app.get("/api/jobs/{job_name}/trials/{trial_name}/artifacts")
2091	    def get_artifacts(
2092	        job_name: str,
2093	        trial_name: str,
2094	        step: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Step name to scope to"),
2095	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2096	        """Get artifacts collected from the trial sandbox."""
2097	        trial_dir = _validate_trial_path(job_name, trial_name)
2098	        if not trial_dir.exists():
2099	            raise HTTPException(
2100	                status_code=404,
2101	                detail=f"Trial '{trial_name}' not found in job '{job_name}'",
2102	            )
2103	
2104	        artifacts_dir = _resolve_step_root(trial_dir, step) / "artifacts"
2105	        if not artifacts_dir.exists():
2106	            return {"files": [], "manifest": None}
2107	
2108	        # Parse manifest.json if present
2109	        manifest = None
2110	        manifest_path = artifacts_dir / "manifest.json"
2111	        if manifest_path.exists():
2112	            try:
2113	                manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())
2114	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
2115	                manifest = None
2116	
2117	        # Scan artifacts directory for files, excluding manifest.json
2118	        files: list[FileInfo] = []
2119	
2120	        def scan_dir(dir_path: Path, relative_base: str = "") -> None:
2121	            try:
2122	                for item in sorted(dir_path.iterdir()):
2123	                    relative_path = (
2124	                        f"{relative_base}/{item.name}" if relative_base else item.name
2125	                    )
2126	                    if item.name == "manifest.json" and not relative_base:
2127	                        continue
2128	                    if item.is_dir():
2129	                        scan_dir(item, relative_path)
2130	                    else:
2131	                        files.append(
2132	                            FileInfo(
2133	                                path=relative_path,
2134	                                name=item.name,
2135	                                is_dir=False,
2136	                                size=item.stat().st_size,
2137	                            )
2138	                        )
2139	            except PermissionError:
2140	                pass
2141	
2142	        scan_dir(artifacts_dir)
2143	        return {"files": files, "manifest": manifest}
2144	
2145	    @app.get("/api/jobs/{job_name}/trials/{trial_name}/agent-logs")
2146	    def get_agent_logs(
2147	        job_name: str,
2148	        trial_name: str,
2149	        step: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Step name to scope to"),
2150	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2151	        """Get agent log files (oracle.txt, setup/stdout.txt, command-*/stdout.txt)."""
2152	        trial_dir = _validate_trial_path(job_name, trial_name)
2153	        if not trial_dir.exists():
2154	            raise HTTPException(
2155	                status_code=404,
2156	                detail=f"Trial '{trial_name}' not found in job '{job_name}'",
2157	            )
2158	
2159	        root = _resolve_step_root(trial_dir, step)
2160	        agent_dir = root / "agent"
2161	        logs: dict[str, Any] = {
2162	            "oracle": None,
2163	            "setup": None,
2164	            "commands": [],
2165	            "summary": None,
2166	            "analysis": None,
2167	        }
2168	
2169	        # Read analysis.md if it exists (always trial-level)
2170	        analysis_path_md = trial_dir / "analysis.md"
2171	        if analysis_path_md.exists():
2172	            try:
2173	                logs["summary"] = analysis_path_md.read_text()
2174	            except Exception:
2175	                logs["summary"] = "[Error reading file]"
2176	
2177	        # Read analysis.json if it exists (structured analysis from harbor analyze)
2178	        analysis_path = trial_dir / "analysis.json"
2179	        if analysis_path.exists():
2180	            try:
2181	                logs["analysis"] = json.loads(analysis_path.read_text())
2182	            except Exception:
2183	                logs["analysis"] = None
2184	
2185	        # Read oracle.txt if it exists
2186	        oracle_path = agent_dir / "oracle.txt"
2187	        if oracle_path.exists():
2188	            try:
2189	                logs["oracle"] = oracle_path.read_text()
2190	            except Exception:
2191	                logs["oracle"] = "[Error reading file]"
2192	
2193	        # Read setup/stdout.txt if it exists
2194	        setup_stdout_path = agent_dir / "setup" / "stdout.txt"
2195	        if setup_stdout_path.exists():
2196	            try:
2197	                logs["setup"] = setup_stdout_path.read_text()
2198	            except Exception:
2199	                logs["setup"] = "[Error reading file]"
2200	
2201	        # Read command-*/stdout.txt files
2202	        i = 0
2203	        while True:
2204	            command_dir = agent_dir / f"command-{i}"
2205	            if not command_dir.exists():
2206	                break
2207	            stdout_path = command_dir / "stdout.txt"
2208	            if stdout_path.exists():
2209	                try:
2210	                    logs["commands"].append(
2211	                        {"index": i, "content": stdout_path.read_text()}
2212	                    )
2213	                except Exception:
2214	                    logs["commands"].append(
2215	                        {"index": i, "content": "[Error reading file]"}
2216	                    )
2217	            i += 1
2218	
2219	        return logs
2220

file.content:
name: Python Tests

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: ["main"]
  push:
    branches: ["main"]
  workflow_dispatch: # Allow manual trigger

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    timeout-minutes: 30
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-2025]

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install the latest version of uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v8.1.0
        with:
          version: "latest"

      - name: Set up Docker
        uses: docker/setup-docker-action@v5
        with:
          version: "latest"

      - name: Set up Docker Compose
        uses: docker/setup-compose-action@v2
        with:
          version: "latest"

      - name: Set up Python 3.13
        run: uv python pin 3.13

      # dspy-rlm runs its REPL in a Deno sandbox; the deterministic dspy-rlm
      # integration test needs Deno on the runner (no secret required).
      - name: Install Deno
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        uses: denoland/setup-deno@v2
        with:
          deno-version: v2.x

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: uv sync --all-packages --all-extras --locked

      - name: Run non-runtime tests with coverage (Linux)
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          uv run pytest tests/ \
            -m "not runtime" \
            --cov=src/harbor \
            --cov-report=term-missing

      - name: Run runtime integration tests with coverage (Linux)
        if: runner.os == 'Linux'
        run: |
          uv run pytest tests/ \
            -m runtime \
            -n 4 \
            --dist load \
            --cov=src/harbor \
            --cov-append \
            --cov-report=xml \
            --cov-report=term-missing

      - name: Run all tests with coverage (Windows)
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        run: |
          uv run pytest tests/ --cov=src/harbor --cov-report=xml --cov-report=term-missing --ignore=tests/unit/agents/installed/test_agent_install_execution.py -m "not runtime and not windows_containers" -k "not test_full_task_mapping"

      - name: Run Windows container integration tests
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        run: |
          uv run pytest tests/ -m "windows_containers" -v

      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
        if: github.event_name == 'push' || github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v6
        with:
          files: ./coverage.xml
          fail_ci_if_error: false
          verbose: true
        env:
          CODECOV_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

1	name: Ruff
2	
3	on:
4	  pull_request:
5	    branches: ["main"]
6	
7	jobs:
8	  lint-and-format:
9	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
10	    timeout-minutes: 10
11	
12	    steps:
13	      - name: Checkout code
14	        uses: actions/checkout@v6
15	        with:
16	          fetch-depth: 0 # Fetch all history to get the base branch
17	          repository: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}
18	          ref: ${{ github.head_ref }} # Checkout the PR branch
19	          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
20	
21	      - name: Install the latest version of uv
22	        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v8.1.0
23	        with:
24	          version: "latest"
25	
26	      - name: Set up Python 3.13
27	        run: uv python pin 3.13

name: Ruff

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: ["main"]

jobs:
  lint-and-format:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0 # Fetch all history to get the base branch
          repository: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}
          ref: ${{ github.head_ref }} # Checkout the PR branch
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Install the latest version of uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v8.1.0
        with:
          version: "latest"

      - name: Set up Python 3.13
        run: uv python pin 3.13

      - name: Run ruff linting
        run: uv run ruff check .

      - name: Run ruff formatting
        run: uv run ruff format --check .

name: Sync Registry to Supabase

on:
  push:
    branches: ["main"]
    paths:
      - "registry.json"
  workflow_dispatch: # Allow manual trigger

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  sync:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v8.1.0

      - name: Sync registry to Supabase
        env:
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY }}
        run: uv run scripts/sync_registry_to_supabase.py

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Overview

`rewardkit` is a lightweight grading toolkit for defining and running verifiers to output rewards. It discovers and runs folder-based "rewards" (grading criteria) against an agent's workspace, producing a structured JSON score. It supports two evaluation modes: **programmatic** (Python criterion functions) and **judge-based** (LLM or agent CLI evaluation via `Criterion` objects).

This is a standalone package. Core dependency is `litellm`; optional extras: `documents` (markitdown for common document file types), `image` (Pillow), `all` (both).

## Commands

```bash
# Install (from this directory)
uv sync

# Run all tests
uv run pytest tests/

# Run a single test file or test
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_runner.py
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_runner.py::TestRunner::test_run_programmatic_e2e

# Lint and format
uv run ruff check --fix .
uv run ruff format .
```

Always run `ruff check --fix` and `ruff format` after changes.

## Public API

Everything is exported from `rewardkit/__init__.py`: `run`, `run_multi`, `discover`, `criterion`, `Reward`, `Criterion`, `Score`, `Binary`, `Likert`, `Numeric`, `LLMJudge`, `AgentJudge`, `compare`, `format_comparison`, `format_trajectory`.

CLI entry point: `rewardkit <tests_dirs...> [--workspace /app] [--output /logs/verifier/reward.json]` (via `__main__.py`). Single dir calls `run()`, multiple dirs calls `run_multi()` and prints a comparison table.

## Architecture

Pipeline: **discover → build Rewards → run → output JSON**.

### Discovery (`runner.py`)

`discover(tests_dir, workspace)` scans a tests directory for reward definitions. Two layouts:

- **Nested**: each subdirectory of `tests_dir` is a separate reward (e.g., `tests/correctness/`, `tests/style/`)
- **Flat**: criteria placed directly in the `tests_dir` root (cannot mix with nested)

Each directory can contain:
- **Python files** (`.py`) — executed at import time; criteria are registered via the `Session` context
- **Judge `.toml` files** — declares `[judge]` + `[[criterion]]` for LLM/agent evaluation. The `[judge]` section uses `judge =` to specify either an LLM model name (e.g. `"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"`) or an agent CLI name (`"claude-code"`, `"codex"`). When `judge =` is an agent, an optional `model =` sets the LLM the agent uses. Agent judges support `isolated = true` in `[judge]`.

Both can coexist in the same directory (programmatic + judge = two Reward objects).

In nested layout, root-level `.py` files are imported first so their `@criterion` factories are available to subdirectory files via the `criteria` module. Root criteria that aren't `shared=True` raise `ValueError` if subdirectories exist (they'd be silently ignored otherwise).

### Session & Criterion Registration (`session.py`, `criteria/`)

A `ContextVar`-based `Session` collects criterion functions during discovery. Registration via:
- `@criterion` decorator (bare or with `description`/`shared` params) — first param is always `workspace: Path`, remaining become factory params
- Built-in criterion functions in `criteria/` module — each is a `@criterion`-decorated factory, accessed as `criteria.file_exists("hello.txt")`

The `criteria/__init__.py` uses `__getattr__` to resolve all criteria from the global `_factory_registry`, allowing user-defined criteria to override built-ins.

**Adding a new built-in criterion**: Create a module in `criteria/`, decorate the function with `@criterion(description=...)`, then add the module name to `_BUILTIN_MODULES` in `criteria/__init__.py`.

Zero-parameter criteria (only `workspace`) auto-register immediately on import. Parameterized criteria that are defined but never called in a discovery context produce a warning.

### Isolation (`isolation.py`)

Workspace isolation uses overlayfs (Linux). When a criterion or agent judge has `isolated=True`, the workspace is mounted as a read-only lower layer with writes going to a temp upper dir. Falls back to fuse-overlayfs if kernel overlay is unavailable.

### Reward Execution (`reward.py`)

`Reward` holds either callable criteria (programmatic) or `Criterion` objects (judge-based) — these are mutually exclusive and validated in `_validate()`. Programmatic criteria receive `workspace: Path` if their signature accepts it. Each criterion runs as a concurrent async task via `asyncio.TaskGroup`. Concurrency is controlled per-type via semaphores passed to `arun()`.

Score aggregation modes on the `Reward.score` property: `weighted_mean` (default), `all_pass`, `any_pass`, `threshold`.

### Judge System (`judges.py`)

- **LLMJudge**: calls LiteLLM with criteria-based system prompt, reads workspace files (text + images via base64) into multimodal content blocks, parses structured JSON response. Supports `files`, `reference`, and `atif_trajectory` fields.
- **AgentJudge**: shells out to `claude` or `codex` CLI, auto-installs if missing. The `codex` backend logs in during `ensure_installed`: with `CODEX_ACCESS_TOKEN` (subscription token) via `codex login --with-access-token`, or with `OPENAI_API_KEY` via `codex login --with-api-key` (codex does not read `OPENAI_API_KEY` on its own). `OPENAI_API_KEY` has priority when both are set, unless `REWARDKIT_FORCE_OAUTH` prefers the access token. `force_oauth()` lives in `agents.py` and is shared with the LLM judge's Anthropic credential resolution.
- Prompt templates in `src/rewardkit/prompts/` (`llm.md`, `agent.md`, `llm_trajectory.md`); custom templates via `prompt_template` in judge `.toml` (must contain `{criteria}` placeholder)
- LLM judges use structured outputs (`json_schema` response format via litellm) and agent judges use `--json-schema` (claude-code) / `--output-schema` (codex) to enforce the response schema. `_build_response_schema()` generates the schema from criteria, using each `OutputFormat.json_schema()` for the score type. `parse_judge_response()` extracts JSON from fenced code blocks or raw braces; raises `ValueError` on unparseable responses.

### Trajectory Support (`trajectory.py`)

Formats ATIF (Agent Trajectory Interchange Format) JSON into compact text for judge prompts. Token-budget-aware: truncates individual content blocks proportionally to fit within model context limits. All steps are always preserved; only content within steps gets truncated.

### Multi-dir Comparison (`compare.py`, `run_multi`)

`run_multi()` runs multiple independent test directories and produces namespaced scores (`"dir/reward"`). `compare()` / `format_comparison()` generate a diff table for overlapping reward names across directories.

### Output

`run()` writes to `/logs/verifier/reward.json` (default) with flat per-reward scores:
```json
{"correctness": 0.75, "structure": 1.0, "quality": 0.6}
```

A separate `reward-details.json` is written alongside with per-criterion breakdown including kind (`programmatic`/`llm`/`agent`), judge config, raw judge output, and any warnings.

### Models (`models.py`)

All models are Pydantic `BaseModel`s with `frozen=True`. Output formats (`Binary`, `Likert`, `Numeric`) implement the `OutputFormat` protocol with `normalize()`, `prompt_fragment()`, and `json_schema()`. The `json_schema()` method returns the JSON Schema fragment for the score field (used by structured output enforcement). `Criterion.name` auto-generates a slug from `description` if not provided.

## Code Conventions

- Use `warnings.warn` (not `logger.warning`) for user-facing warnings about criterion behavior (e.g. out-of-range scores, unexpected formats). This ensures users see the warning even without logging configured.
- **Agent backends must not store mutable per-call state on the instance.** `get_agent()` returns a fresh instance per call because agent judges run concurrently via `asyncio.TaskGroup`. Any temp files or call-specific state must live on the instance returned by `get_agent()`, never on a shared singleton.

## Testing Conventions

- All tests are in `tests/unit/` — no integration tests
- `conftest.py` provides two autouse fixtures:
  - `_fresh_session`: resets session and `_factory_registry` per test (saves/restores registry state)
  - `_fake_overlayfs`: patches `_Overlay.mount` and `_Overlay.cleanup` to simulate overlayfs via `shutil.copytree` — this runs automatically on macOS/non-root where real overlayfs is unavailable
- No special markers needed; all tests run with `pytest`

content.subject: Research core architecture & execution lifecycle
content.description:
Investigate Harbor's core abstractions and how a run flows end-to-end. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/agents/base.py` (BaseAgent abstract class), `agents/factory.py`
- `src/harbor/environments/base.py` (BaseEnvironment), `environments/factory.py`
- `src/harbor/orchestrators/` — how trials are orchestrated (concurrency, retries)
- `src/harbor/trial/` and `src/harbor/job.py` — trial vs job models and lifecycle
- `src/harbor/models/` — Pydantic v2 data models (agent, job, task, trial, metric, verifier)
- The async execution lifecycle: environment.start → agent.setup → agent.run → verifier.verify → environment.stop
- `src/harbor/__init__.py` lazy-import pattern

Deliver: a structured summary (key files with paths, the main classes/interfaces, the end-to-end flow of `harbor run`, concurrency model, and notable design patterns). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research agents & environment backends
content.description:
Survey Harbor's agent implementations and execution environment backends. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/agents/installed/` — the 18+ built-in agents (claude-code, openhands, codex, aider, goose, gemini-cli, etc.): how they subclass BaseAgent, setup/run patterns, env var handling, `--ae`/`--agent-env`
- `src/harbor/agents/installed/base.py` (BaseInstalledAgent) if present, `factory.py`, registration in `models/agent/name.py` (AgentName enum)
- `src/harbor/agents/terminus_2/` and internal Terminus agents
- `src/harbor/environments/` backends: docker, daytona, e2b, modal, runloop, apple_container, gke, openshift, novita — what each provides, how BaseEnvironment is implemented (start/stop/exec/upload), EnvironmentType enum & factory
- SUPPORTS_ATIF and SUPPORTS_WINDOWS flags

Deliver: a structured summary (catalog of agents with one-line each, catalog of environments, how to add a new agent/environment, key shared patterns). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research the CLI & command surface
content.description:
Survey Harbor's command-line interface and user-facing workflows. Focus on:
- `src/harbor/cli/main.py` — entry point, how command groups are registered (app.add_typer), the `harbor`/`hr`/`hb` scripts
- Each command module in `src/harbor/cli/`: run/jobs/datasets/trials/tasks/traces/sweeps/adapters/adapter_wizard/publish/analyze/cache/view, plus `admin/`, `annotator/`, `debug_checker/`, `quality_checker/`, `template-*`
- The primary `harbor run` command — its flags (--dataset, --agent, --model, --n-concurrent, --ae, --agent-import-path combined into --agent per recent commit), how it maps to jobs/trials
- Sweeps (parameter sweeps), traces viewing, analysis backend
- Recently added flags: agent/verifier log-filter flags on `harbor trial start`

Deliver: a structured summary (the command tree, the main user workflows from CLI, key flags, how the CLI maps onto the core models). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research benchmarks, verifier, metrics & registry
content.description:
Survey Harbor's benchmark adapter system, verification, metrics, and dataset registry. Focus on:
- `adapters/` — how the 50+ adapters convert external benchmarks (SWE-Bench, Terminal-Bench, Aider Polyglot, LiveCodeBench, etc.) to Harbor task format; the adapter.py/run_adapter.py/template structure
- The task format: task.toml, instruction.md, environment/, tests/ (test.sh writes reward to /logs/verifier/reward.txt or reward.json), solution/
- `src/harbor/verifier/` — the test verification system; `src/harbor/models/verifier/`
- `src/harbor/metrics/` — metrics collection incl. pass@k (recently added unit tests, commit 496293b0)
- `src/harbor/registry/` and `registry.json` (13.9MB!) + `src/harbor/dataset/` — dataset registry, how datasets are listed/resolved (`harbor datasets list`, `dataset@version` syntax)
- `src/harbor/models/trajectories/` — the ATIF (Agent Trajectory Interchange Format)
- `src/harbor/leaderboard/`

Deliver: a structured summary (how adapters work, the canonical task format, the verifier contract, metrics incl pass@k, how the registry/datasets work, ATIF overview). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

content.subject: Research web apps, storage, packages & CI/CD
content.description:
Survey Harbor's web apps, storage, publishing, packages, and CI/CD. Focus on:
- `apps/viewer/` — the results viewer web app (React Router + Vite): what it visualizes, tech stack, how it gets data
- `docs/` — the documentation site (Next.js + Fumadocs)
- `src/harbor/storage/` (Supabase backends), `src/harbor/db/`, `src/harbor/auth/` (OAuth callback server) — note the Supabase/PostgREST pagination rules from AGENTS.md
- `src/harbor/publisher/` — package publishing & registry DB; `src/harbor/upload/` and `src/harbor/download/`
- `packages/` — the uv workspace members (harbor-rewardkit, harbor-langsmith); how `pyproject.toml [tool.uv.workspace]` ties them
- `.github/workflows/` — CI/CD (pytest, ruff, ty, claude-code-review, sync-registry, adapter-review, etc.)
- `examples/training/` and the RL optimization claim — how Harbor generates rollouts for RL
- `rfcs/` and `skills/`

Deliver: a structured summary (viewer app overview, docs, storage/auth model, publisher/registry, workspace packages, CI matrix, RL/training hooks). Cite file paths. Report findings back to the team lead via SendMessage when done, then mark this task completed.

file.content:
name: Claude Code

on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]
  issues:
    types: [opened, assigned]
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  claude:
    if: |
      (github.event_name == 'issue_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review' && contains(github.event.review.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'issues' && (contains(github.event.issue.body, '@claude') || contains(github.event.issue.title, '@claude')))
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
      issues: read
      id-token: write
      actions: read # Required for Claude to read CI results on PRs
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Run Claude Code
        id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

          # This is an optional setting that allows Claude to read CI results on PRs
          additional_permissions: |
            actions: read
          
          # Optional: Specify model (defaults to Claude Sonnet 4, uncomment for Claude Opus 4.1)
          # model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"
          
          # Optional: Customize the trigger phrase (default: @claude)
          # trigger_phrase: "/claude"
          
          # Optional: Trigger when specific user is assigned to an issue
          # assignee_trigger: "claude-bot"
          
          # Optional: Allow Claude to run specific commands
          # allowed_tools: "Bash(npm install),Bash(npm run build),Bash(npm run test:*),Bash(npm run lint:*)"
          
          # Optional: Add custom instructions for Claude to customize its behavior for your project
          # custom_instructions: |
          #   Follow our coding standards
          #   Ensure all new code has tests
          #   Use TypeScript for new files
          
          # Optional: Custom environment variables for Claude
          # claude_env: |
          #   NODE_ENV: test

name: Claude Code Review

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  # pull_request:
  #   types: [opened, synchronize]
  # Optional: Only run on specific file changes
  # paths:
  #   - "src/**/*.ts"
  #   - "src/**/*.tsx"
  #   - "src/**/*.js"
  #   - "src/**/*.jsx"

jobs:
  claude-review:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

      contents: read

      issues: read
      id-token: write

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Run Claude Code Review
        id: claude-review
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

          # Optional: Specify model (defaults to Claude Sonnet 4, uncomment for Claude Opus 4.1)
          # model: "claude-opus-4-1-20250805"

          # Direct prompt for automated review (no @claude mention needed)
          prompt: |
            Please review this pull request and provide feedback on:
            - Code quality and best practices
            - Potential bugs or issues
            - Performance considerations
            - Security concerns
            - Test coverage

            Be constructive and helpful in your feedback.

          # Optional: Use sticky comments to make Claude reuse the same comment on subsequent pushes to the same PR
          # use_sticky_comment: true

          # Optional: Customize review based on file types
          # prompt: |
          #   Review this PR focusing on:
          #   - For TypeScript files: Type safety and proper interface usage
          #   - For API endpoints: Security, input validation, and error handling
          #   - For React components: Performance, accessibility, and best practices
          #   - For tests: Coverage, edge cases, and test quality

          # Optional: Different prompts for different authors

          #   ${{ github.event.pull_request.author_association == 'FIRST_TIME_CONTRIBUTOR' &&
          #   'Welcome! Please review this PR from a first-time contributor. Be encouraging and provide detailed explanations for any suggestions.' ||
          #   'Please provide a thorough code review focusing on our coding standards and best practices.' }}

          # Optional: Add specific tools for running tests or linting
          # allowed_tools: "Bash(npm run test),Bash(npm run lint),Bash(npm run typecheck)"

          # Optional: Skip review for certain conditions
          # if: |
          #   !contains(github.event.pull_request.title, '[skip-review]') &&
          #   !contains(github.event.pull_request.title, '[WIP]')

file.content:
name: Check registry.json format

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: ["main"]
    paths:
      - "registry.json"

jobs:
  check-format:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Validate registry.json format (indent=2, no duplicates)
        run: |
          python3 -c "
          import json, sys

          with open('registry.json') as f:
              raw = f.read()
              f.seek(0)
              data = json.load(f)

          expected = json.dumps(data, indent=2) + '\n'
          if raw != expected:
              print('::error::registry.json formatting does not match indent=2. Please reformat.')
              sys.exit(1)

          seen = set()
          for ds in data:
              key = (ds['name'], ds['version'])
              if key in seen:
                  print(f'::error::Duplicate dataset: {key[0]}@{key[1]}')
                  sys.exit(1)
              seen.add(key)

          for ds in data:
              for t in ds.get('tasks', []):
                  if not t.get('git_url') or not t.get('git_commit_id'):
                      print(f'::error::Task {t.get(\"name\")} in {ds[\"name\"]} missing git_url or git_commit_id')
                      sys.exit(1)

          print(f'registry.json OK: {len(data)} datasets, indent=2, no duplicates')
          "

file.content:
name: PR Labeler

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      pr_number:
        description: PR number to label
        required: true
        type: string

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  labeler:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Apply area labels
        uses: actions/labeler@v6
        with:
          sync-labels: true
          pr-number: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.pr_number || github.event.pull_request.number }}
