289	            environment_config=config.environment.model_dump(mode="json"),
290	            instruction=instruction,
291	            readme=readme,
292	            files=file_rows,
293	            visibility=visibility,
294	            multi_step_reward_strategy=(
295	                (
296	                    config.multi_step_reward_strategy or MultiStepRewardStrategy.MEAN
297	                ).value
298	                if config.steps
299	                else None
300	            ),
301	            healthcheck_config=(
302	                config.environment.healthcheck.model_dump(mode="json")
303	                if config.environment.healthcheck
304	                else None
305	            ),
306	            steps=(_build_step_payload(paths, config.steps) if config.steps else None),
307	        )
308	
309	        rpc_time = time.monotonic() - rpc_start
310	        created = rpc_result.get("created", True)
311	        revision = rpc_result.get("revision") if created else None
312	
313	        return PublishResult(
314	            name=config.task.name,
315	            content_hash=content_hash,
316	            archive_path=remote_path,
317	            file_count=len(files),
318	            archive_size_bytes=archive_size,
319	            build_time_sec=round(build_time, 3),
320	            upload_time_sec=round(upload_time, 3),
321	            rpc_time_sec=round(rpc_time, 3),
322	            skipped=skipped,
323	            revision=revision,
324	            tags=sorted(applied_tags),
325	            db_skipped=not created,
326	        )
327	
328	    async def publish_tasks(
329	        self,
330	        task_dirs: list[Path],
331	        *,
332	        max_concurrency: int = 100,
333	        tags: set[str] | None = None,
334	        visibility: str = "public",
335	        on_task_upload_start: Callable[[Path], Any] | None = None,
336	        on_task_upload_complete: Callable[[Path, PublishResult], Any] | None = None,
337	    ) -> BatchPublishResult:
338	        if not task_dirs:
339	            return BatchPublishResult(results=[], total_time_sec=0.0)
340	
341	        start = time.monotonic()
342	        results: list[PublishResult | None] = [None] * len(task_dirs)
343	        semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrency)
344	
345	        async def _publish(index: int, task_dir: Path) -> None:
346	            async with semaphore:
347	                try:
348	                    if on_task_upload_start:
349	                        on_task_upload_start(task_dir)
350	                    result = await self.publish_task(
351	                        task_dir, tags=tags, visibility=visibility
352	                    )
353	                    results[index] = result
354	                    if on_task_upload_complete:
355	                        on_task_upload_complete(task_dir, result)
356	                except Exception as exc:
357	                    exc.add_note(f"while publishing task {task_dir}")
358	                    raise
359	
360	        async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as tg:
361	            for i, task_dir in enumerate(task_dirs):
362	                tg.create_task(_publish(i, task_dir))
363	
364	        return BatchPublishResult(
365	            results=[r for r in results if r is not None],
366	            total_time_sec=round(time.monotonic() - start, 3),
367	        )
368	
369	    async def publish_dataset(
370	        self,
371	        dataset_dir: Path,
372	        tags: set[str] | None = None,
373	        visibility: str = "public",
374	        promote_tasks: bool = False,
375	    ) -> DatasetPublishResult:
376	        paths = DatasetPaths(dataset_dir)
377	        if not paths.manifest_path.exists():
378	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"dataset.toml not found in {dataset_dir}")
379	
380	        manifest = DatasetManifest.from_toml_file(paths.manifest_path)
381	        db = self.registry_db
382	
383	        # Ensure the org exists before uploading (storage RLS requires membership)
384	        await self.registry_db.ensure_org(manifest.dataset.org)
385	
386	        # Upload dataset-level files to storage before RPC call
387	        file_infos: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
388	        for file_ref in manifest.files:
389	            file_path = dataset_dir / file_ref.path
390	            if not file_path.exists():
391	                raise FileNotFoundError(
392	                    f"Dataset file '{file_ref.path}' not found in {dataset_dir}"
393	                )
394	            result = await self.publish_file(manifest.dataset.name, file_path)
395	            file_infos.append(
396	                {
397	                    "path": file_ref.path,
398	                    "content_hash": result.content_hash,
399	                    "size_bytes": result.file_size_bytes,
400	                    "storage_path": result.remote_path,
401	                }
402	            )
403	
404	        readme = paths.readme_path.read_text() if paths.readme_path.exists() else None
405	
406	        applied_tags = {"latest"} | (tags or set())
407	
408	        task_refs = [
409	            {"org": ref.org, "name": ref.short_name, "digest": ref.digest}
410	            for ref in manifest.tasks
411	        ]
412	
413	        rpc_start = time.monotonic()
414	        rpc_result = await db.publish_dataset_version(
415	            org=manifest.dataset.org,
416	            name=manifest.dataset.short_name,
417	            tags=sorted(applied_tags),
418	            description=manifest.dataset.description,
419	            authors=[a.model_dump(mode="json") for a in manifest.dataset.authors],
420	            tasks=task_refs,
421	            files=file_infos,
422	            readme=readme,
423	            visibility=visibility,
424	            promote_tasks=promote_tasks,
425	        )
426	        rpc_time = time.monotonic() - rpc_start
427	
428	        created = rpc_result.get("created", True)
429	        content_hash = rpc_result.get("content_hash", "")
430	
431	        return DatasetPublishResult(
432	            name=manifest.dataset.name,
433	            content_hash=content_hash,
434	            revision=rpc_result.get("revision", 0),
435	            task_count=manifest.task_count,
436	            file_count=len(file_infos),
437	            skipped=not created,

441	        )

import asyncio
import hashlib
import ssl
import tarfile
import tempfile
import time
from collections.abc import Callable
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import httpx
from pydantic import BaseModel
from storage3.exceptions import StorageApiError
from tenacity import (
    retry,
    retry_if_exception,
    stop_after_attempt,
    wait_exponential,
)

from harbor.auth.client import reset_client
from harbor.constants import ARCHIVE_FILENAME
from harbor.models.dataset.manifest import DatasetManifest
from harbor.models.dataset.paths import DatasetPaths
from harbor.models.task.config import (
    MultiStepRewardStrategy,
    StepConfig,
    TaskConfig,
)
from harbor.models.task.paths import TaskPaths
from harbor.models.task.task import Task
from harbor.publisher.packager import Packager
from harbor.db.client import RegistryDB
from harbor.storage.supabase import SupabaseStorage

PACKAGE_DIR = "packages"
_NON_RETRYABLE_LOCAL_OS_ERRORS = (
    FileNotFoundError,
    IsADirectoryError,
    NotADirectoryError,
    PermissionError,
)


def _is_retryable_publish_error(exc: BaseException) -> bool:
    if isinstance(exc, _NON_RETRYABLE_LOCAL_OS_ERRORS):
        return False
    return isinstance(exc, (httpx.RequestError, ssl.SSLError, OSError))


def _build_step_payload(
    paths: TaskPaths, steps: list[StepConfig]
) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for i, step in enumerate(steps):
        instruction_path = paths.steps_dir / step.name / "instruction.md"
        rows.append(
            {
                "step_index": i,
                "name": step.name,
                "instruction": instruction_path.read_text(),
                "agent_config": step.agent.model_dump(mode="json"),
                "verifier_config": step.verifier.model_dump(mode="json"),
                "healthcheck_config": (
                    step.healthcheck.model_dump(mode="json")
                    if step.healthcheck
                    else None
                ),
                "min_reward": step.min_reward,
            }
        )
    return rows


class PublishResult(BaseModel):
    name: str
    content_hash: str
    archive_path: str
    file_count: int
    archive_size_bytes: int
    build_time_sec: float
    upload_time_sec: float
    rpc_time_sec: float = 0.0
    skipped: bool = False
    revision: int | None = None
    tags: list[str] | None = None
    db_skipped: bool = False


class BatchPublishResult(BaseModel):
    results: list[PublishResult]
    total_time_sec: float


class DatasetPublishResult(BaseModel):
    name: str
    content_hash: str
    revision: int
    task_count: int
    file_count: int
    skipped: bool = False
    db_skipped: bool = False
    rpc_time_sec: float = 0.0
    tags: list[str] | None = None


class FilePublishResult(BaseModel):
    content_hash: str
    remote_path: str
    file_size_bytes: int
    upload_time_sec: float
    skipped: bool = False


class Publisher:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.storage = SupabaseStorage()
        self.registry_db = RegistryDB()

    @staticmethod
    def _create_archive(task_dir: Path, files: list[Path], dest: Path) -> None:
        with tarfile.open(dest, "w:gz") as tar:
            for f in files:
                rel = f.relative_to(task_dir).as_posix()
                info = tarfile.TarInfo(name=rel)
                info.size = f.stat().st_size
                info.uid = 0
                info.gid = 0
                info.uname = ""
                info.gname = ""
                info.mtime = 0
                info.mode = 0o644
                with f.open("rb") as handle:
                    tar.addfile(info, handle)

    async def publish_file(
        self, package_name: str, file_path: Path
    ) -> FilePublishResult:
        content_hash = Packager.compute_file_hash(file_path)
        remote_path = f"{PACKAGE_DIR}/{package_name}/{content_hash}/{file_path.name}"
        file_size = file_path.stat().st_size
        skipped = False
        upload_start = time.monotonic()
        try:
            await self.storage.upload_file(file_path, remote_path)
        except StorageApiError as exc:
            if exc.status in (409, "409"):
                skipped = True
            elif exc.status in (403, "403"):
                raise PermissionError(
                    "You don't have permission to publish to this package. "
                    "Either the organization doesn't exist or you are not a member with publish rights."
                ) from exc
            else:
                raise
        upload_time = time.monotonic() - upload_start
        return FilePublishResult(
            content_hash=content_hash,
            remote_path=remote_path,
            file_size_bytes=file_size,
            upload_time_sec=round(upload_time, 3),
            skipped=skipped,
        )

    @retry(
        retry=retry_if_exception(_is_retryable_publish_error),
        stop=stop_after_attempt(3),
        wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=1, max=8),
        before_sleep=lambda _: reset_client(),
        reraise=True,
    )
    async def publish_task(
        self,
        task_dir: Path,
        tags: set[str] | None = None,
        visibility: str = "public",
    ) -> PublishResult:
        paths = TaskPaths(task_dir)
        if not paths.config_path.exists():
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"task.toml not found in {task_dir}")

        config = TaskConfig.model_validate_toml(paths.config_path.read_text())
        if config.task is None:
            raise ValueError("task.toml must contain a [task] section with a name")

        if not paths.environment_dir.exists():
            raise ValueError(f"Task directory {task_dir} is missing environment/.")

        try:
            Task(task_dir)
        except FileNotFoundError as e:
            raise ValueError(str(e)) from e

        # Ensure the org exists before uploading (storage RLS requires membership)
        await self.registry_db.ensure_org(config.task.org)

        build_start = time.monotonic()
        content_hash, files = Packager.compute_content_hash(task_dir)

        # Preflight: skip archive creation and upload if version already exists
        if await self.registry_db.task_version_exists(
            config.task.org, config.task.short_name, content_hash
        ):
            build_time = time.monotonic() - build_start
            applied_tags = {"latest"} | (tags or set())
            return PublishResult(
                name=config.task.name,
                content_hash=content_hash,
                archive_path=f"{PACKAGE_DIR}/{config.task.name}/{content_hash}/{ARCHIVE_FILENAME}",
                file_count=len(files),
                archive_size_bytes=0,
                build_time_sec=round(build_time, 3),
                upload_time_sec=0.0,
                rpc_time_sec=0.0,
                skipped=True,
                revision=None,
                tags=sorted(applied_tags),
                db_skipped=True,
            )

        skipped = False
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            archive_path = Path(tmp) / ARCHIVE_FILENAME
            self._create_archive(task_dir, files, archive_path)
            archive_size = archive_path.stat().st_size
            build_time = time.monotonic() - build_start

            remote_path = (
                f"{PACKAGE_DIR}/{config.task.name}/{content_hash}/{ARCHIVE_FILENAME}"
            )
            upload_start = time.monotonic()
            try:
                await self.storage.upload_file(archive_path, remote_path)
            except StorageApiError as exc:
                if exc.status in (409, "409"):
                    skipped = True
                elif exc.status in (403, "403"):
                    raise PermissionError(
                        "You don't have permission to publish to this package. "
                        "Either the organization doesn't exist or you are not a member with publish rights."
                    ) from exc
                else:
                    raise
            upload_time = time.monotonic() - upload_start

        # DB registration via RPC
        db = self.registry_db

        instruction: str | None
        if paths.instruction_path.exists():
            instruction = paths.instruction_path.read_text()
        elif config.steps:
            instruction = None
        else:
            instruction = ""

        readme = ""
        if paths.readme_path.exists():
            readme = paths.readme_path.read_text()

        applied_tags = {"latest"} | (tags or set())

        file_rows = []
        for f in files:
            rel = f.relative_to(task_dir).as_posix()
            file_data = f.read_bytes()
            fhash = f"{hashlib.sha256(file_data).hexdigest()}"
            file_rows.append(
                {
                    "path": rel,
                    "content_hash": fhash,
                    "size_bytes": len(file_data),
                }
            )

        rpc_start = time.monotonic()
        rpc_result = await db.publish_task_version(
            org=config.task.org,
            name=config.task.short_name,
            tags=sorted(applied_tags),
            content_hash=content_hash,
            archive_path=remote_path,
            description=config.task.description,
            authors=[a.model_dump(mode="json") for a in config.task.authors],
            keywords=config.task.keywords,
            metadata=config.metadata,
            verifier_config=config.verifier.model_dump(mode="json"),
            agent_config=config.agent.model_dump(mode="json"),
            environment_config=config.environment.model_dump(mode="json"),
            instruction=instruction,
            readme=readme,
            files=file_rows,
            visibility=visibility,
            multi_step_reward_strategy=(
                (
                    config.multi_step_reward_strategy or MultiStepRewardStrategy.MEAN
                ).value
                if config.steps
                else None
            ),
            healthcheck_config=(
                config.environment.healthcheck.model_dump(mode="json")
                if config.environment.healthcheck
                else None
            ),
            steps=(_build_step_payload(paths, config.steps) if config.steps else None),
        )

        rpc_time = time.monotonic() - rpc_start
        created = rpc_result.get("created", True)
        revision = rpc_result.get("revision") if created else None

        return PublishResult(
            name=config.task.name,
            content_hash=content_hash,
            archive_path=remote_path,
            file_count=len(files),
            archive_size_bytes=archive_size,
            build_time_sec=round(build_time, 3),
            upload_time_sec=round(upload_time, 3),
            rpc_time_sec=round(rpc_time, 3),
            skipped=skipped,
            revision=revision,
            tags=sorted(applied_tags),
            db_skipped=not created,
        )

    async def publish_tasks(
        self,
        task_dirs: list[Path],
        *,
        max_concurrency: int = 100,
        tags: set[str] | None = None,
        visibility: str = "public",
        on_task_upload_start: Callable[[Path], Any] | None = None,
        on_task_upload_complete: Callable[[Path, PublishResult], Any] | None = None,
    ) -> BatchPublishResult:
        if not task_dirs:
            return BatchPublishResult(results=[], total_time_sec=0.0)

        start = time.monotonic()
        results: list[PublishResult | None] = [None] * len(task_dirs)
        semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrency)

        async def _publish(index: int, task_dir: Path) -> None:
            async with semaphore:
                try:
                    if on_task_upload_start:
                        on_task_upload_start(task_dir)
                    result = await self.publish_task(
                        task_dir, tags=tags, visibility=visibility
                    )
                    results[index] = result
                    if on_task_upload_complete:
                        on_task_upload_complete(task_dir, result)
                except Exception as exc:
                    exc.add_note(f"while publishing task {task_dir}")
                    raise

        async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as tg:
            for i, task_dir in enumerate(task_dirs):
                tg.create_task(_publish(i, task_dir))

        return BatchPublishResult(
            results=[r for r in results if r is not None],
            total_time_sec=round(time.monotonic() - start, 3),
        )

    async def publish_dataset(
        self,
        dataset_dir: Path,
        tags: set[str] | None = None,
        visibility: str = "public",
        promote_tasks: bool = False,
    ) -> DatasetPublishResult:
        paths = DatasetPaths(dataset_dir)
        if not paths.manifest_path.exists():
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"dataset.toml not found in {dataset_dir}")

        manifest = DatasetManifest.from_toml_file(paths.manifest_path)
        db = self.registry_db

        # Ensure the org exists before uploading (storage RLS requires membership)
        await self.registry_db.ensure_org(manifest.dataset.org)

        # Upload dataset-level files to storage before RPC call
        file_infos: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        for file_ref in manifest.files:
            file_path = dataset_dir / file_ref.path
            if not file_path.exists():
                raise FileNotFoundError(
                    f"Dataset file '{file_ref.path}' not found in {dataset_dir}"
                )
            result = await self.publish_file(manifest.dataset.name, file_path)
            file_infos.append(
                {
                    "path": file_ref.path,
                    "content_hash": result.content_hash,
                    "size_bytes": result.file_size_bytes,
                    "storage_path": result.remote_path,
                }
            )

        readme = paths.readme_path.read_text() if paths.readme_path.exists() else None

        applied_tags = {"latest"} | (tags or set())

        task_refs = [
            {"org": ref.org, "name": ref.short_name, "digest": ref.digest}
            for ref in manifest.tasks
        ]

        rpc_start = time.monotonic()
        rpc_result = await db.publish_dataset_version(
            org=manifest.dataset.org,
            name=manifest.dataset.short_name,
            tags=sorted(applied_tags),
            description=manifest.dataset.description,
            authors=[a.model_dump(mode="json") for a in manifest.dataset.authors],
            tasks=task_refs,
            files=file_infos,
            readme=readme,
            visibility=visibility,
            promote_tasks=promote_tasks,
        )
        rpc_time = time.monotonic() - rpc_start

        created = rpc_result.get("created", True)
        content_hash = rpc_result.get("content_hash", "")

        return DatasetPublishResult(
            name=manifest.dataset.name,
            content_hash=content_hash,
            revision=rpc_result.get("revision", 0),
            task_count=manifest.task_count,
            file_count=len(file_infos),
            skipped=not created,
            db_skipped=not created,
            rpc_time_sec=round(rpc_time, 3),
            tags=sorted(applied_tags),
        )

content.content:
1	"""Core orchestrator for uploading job results to Supabase."""
2	
3	import asyncio
4	import logging
5	import tarfile
6	import time
7	from collections.abc import Callable
8	from datetime import datetime
9	from io import BytesIO
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import Any
12	from uuid import UUID
13	
14	from pydantic import BaseModel
15	
16	from harbor.db.types import PublicJobVisibility
17	from harbor.models.job.config import JobConfig
18	from harbor.models.job.result import JobResult
19	from harbor.models.trial.result import TrialResult
20	from harbor.upload.db_client import UploadDB
21	from harbor.upload.storage import UploadStorage
22	
23	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
24	
25	
26	class TrialUploadResult(BaseModel):
27	    trial_name: str
28	    task_name: str
29	    reward: float | int | None = None
30	    archive_size_bytes: int = 0
31	    upload_time_sec: float = 0.0
32	    skipped: bool = False
33	    error: str | None = None
34	
35	
36	class JobStartResult(BaseModel):
37	    """Result of :meth:`Uploader.start_job`.
38	
39	    Carries the caches the streaming-upload hook handler needs for each
40	    per-trial upload (so it doesn't re-upsert agent/model per trial) and a
41	    sentinel indicating whether the row existed server-side already.
42	    """
43	
44	    job_id: UUID
45	    visibility: PublicJobVisibility
46	    already_existed: bool
47	    shared_orgs: list[str] = []
48	    shared_users: list[str] = []
49	    # agent_cache: (name, version) -> agent_id
50	    agent_cache: dict[tuple[str, str], str] = {}
51	    # model_cache: (name, provider) -> model_id. Provider is None when the
52	    # user ran with a bare model name (no "<provider>/" prefix) — the cache
53	    # key has to preserve that distinction so a user running both
54	    # `-m gpt-5.4` and `-m openai/gpt-5.4` in the same job doesn't collide
55	    # into one cache entry.
56	    model_cache: dict[tuple[str, str | None], str] = {}
57	
58	
59	class JobUploadResult(BaseModel):
60	    job_name: str
61	    job_id: str
62	    visibility: PublicJobVisibility
63	    shared_orgs: list[str] = []
64	    shared_users: list[str] = []
65	    job_already_existed: bool = False
66	    n_trials_uploaded: int = 0
67	    n_trials_skipped: int = 0
68	    n_trials_failed: int = 0
69	    total_time_sec: float = 0.0
70	    trial_results: list[TrialUploadResult] = []
71	
72	
73	# Allowlist of entries included in each archive. Everything else in the
74	# directory is intentionally left out — random scratch files, editor
75	# metadata, or secrets a user happens to drop in the job_dir don't leak into
76	# shared artifacts. A missing entry is silently skipped (most are optional).
77	#
78	# Per-trial archive: self-contained trial subdir. Consumed by the viewer UI
79	# (artifact browser) and by `harbor trial download`.
80	_TRIAL_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES: tuple[str, ...] = (
81	    "config.json",
82	    "result.json",
83	    "analysis.md",
84	    "agent",
85	    "verifier",
86	    "artifacts",
87	    "trial.log",
88	    "exception.txt",
89	)
90	
91	# Per-step archive entries under ``steps/{step_name}/``. Multi-step trials
92	# may leave other task/setup files under the step tree; only runtime outputs
93	# should be uploaded as trial artifacts.
94	_STEP_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES: tuple[str, ...] = (
95	    "agent",
96	    "verifier",
97	    "artifacts",
98	)
99	
100	# Per-job archive: job-level allowlist + every trial subdir, each filtered
101	# through `_TRIAL_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES`. Consumed by `harbor job download`.
102	_JOB_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES: tuple[str, ...] = (
103	    "config.json",
104	    "lock.json",
105	    "result.json",
106	    "analysis.md",
107	    "job.log",
108	)
109	
110	
111	class Uploader:
112	    def __init__(self) -> None:
113	        self.db = UploadDB()
114	        self.storage = UploadStorage()
115	        self._agent_cache_lock = asyncio.Lock()
116	        self._model_cache_lock = asyncio.Lock()
117	
118	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
119	    # Streaming primitives
120	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
121	    #
122	    # `upload_job` below composes these three for the standalone CLI.
123	    # `harbor run --upload` uses them individually:
124	    #   1. `start_job(...)` at run start — inserts the job row with
125	    #      `archive_path=NULL` and primes the agent/model caches.
126	    #   2. `upload_single_trial(...)` registered as a `TrialEvent.END` hook —
127	    #      runs once per trial as they finish, in parallel with the next
128	    #      trial's work.
129	    #   3. `finalize_job(...)` at run end — builds the job archive, uploads
130	    #      it, writes `archive_path` + `finished_at` onto the row.
131	    # A crash between 1 and 3 leaves the row in "in-progress" state
132	    # (archive_path NULL); `harbor upload <job_dir>` later is an idempotent
133	    # sweep that fills any missing trials and finalizes if needed.
134	
135	    async def start_job(
136	        self,
137	        *,
138	        job_id: UUID,
139	        job_name: str,
140	        started_at: datetime,
141	        config: dict[str, Any],
142	        visibility: PublicJobVisibility | None = None,
143	        share_orgs: list[str] | None = None,
144	        share_users: list[str] | None = None,
145	        confirm_non_member_orgs: bool = False,
146	        n_planned_trials: int | None = None,
147	    ) -> JobStartResult:
148	        """Insert the job row (or detect an existing one) from an explicit
149	        spec — no disk reads.
150	
151	        The spec-based signature lets `harbor run --upload` call this before
152	        the run produces any ``result.json`` files. The standalone
153	        ``upload_job`` path reads ``result.json`` + ``config.json`` itself
154	        and then calls this with the extracted fields.
155	
156	        ``archive_path`` / ``finished_at`` / ``log_path`` are left ``NULL`` —
157	        :meth:`finalize_job` writes them at end-of-run. The returned caches
158	        start empty; :meth:`_upload_single_trial` lazily upserts agents +
159	        models as it sees them.
160	
161	        Visibility rules:
162	        - ``None`` + new row → default ``"private"``
163	        - ``None`` + existing row → keep server value
164	        - explicit + existing row → ``update_job_visibility`` if different
165	        """
166	        await self.db.get_user_id()  # auth check
167	
168	        existing_visibility = await self.db.get_job_visibility(job_id)
169	        already_existed = existing_visibility is not None
170	
171	        if not already_existed:
172	            effective_visibility: PublicJobVisibility = visibility or "private"
173	            await self.db.insert_job(
174	                id=job_id,
175	                job_name=job_name,
176	                started_at=started_at,
177	                finished_at=None,  # filled by finalize_job
178	                config=config,
179	                log_path=None,  # filled by finalize_job
180	                archive_path=None,  # filled by finalize_job
181	                visibility=effective_visibility,
182	                n_planned_trials=n_planned_trials,
183	            )
184	        else:
185	            assert existing_visibility is not None
186	            if visibility is not None and visibility != existing_visibility:
187	                await self.db.update_job_visibility(job_id, visibility)
188	                effective_visibility = visibility
189	                logger.debug(
190	                    "Job %s already existed; flipped visibility %s → %s.",
191	                    job_id,
192	                    existing_visibility,
193	                    visibility,
194	                )
195	            else:
196	                effective_visibility = existing_visibility
197	
198	        shared_orgs: list[str] = []
199	        shared_users: list[str] = []
200	        if share_orgs or share_users:
201	            shares = await self.db.add_job_shares(
202	                job_id=job_id,
203	                org_names=share_orgs or [],
204	                usernames=share_users or [],
205	                confirm_non_member_orgs=confirm_non_member_orgs,
206	            )
207	            shared_orgs = [
208	                str(org.get("name"))
209	                for org in shares.get("orgs", [])
210	                if isinstance(org, dict) and org.get("name")
211	            ]
212	            shared_users = [
213	                str(user.get("github_username") or user.get("id"))
214	                for user in shares.get("users", [])
215	                if isinstance(user, dict)
216	                and (user.get("github_username") or user.get("id"))
217	            ]
218	
219	        return JobStartResult(
220	            job_id=job_id,
221	            visibility=effective_visibility,
222	            already_existed=already_existed,
223	            shared_orgs=shared_orgs,
224	            shared_users=shared_users,
225	            agent_cache={},
226	            model_cache={},
227	        )
228	
229	    async def upload_single_trial(
230	        self,
231	        *,
232	        trial_result: TrialResult,
233	        trial_dir: Path | None,
234	        job_id: UUID,
235	        agent_cache: dict[tuple[str, str], str],
236	        model_cache: dict[tuple[str, str | None], str],
237	    ) -> TrialUploadResult:
238	        """Upload one trial's archive + trajectory and insert its DB rows.
239	
240	        Public API so `harbor run --upload`'s `on_trial_ended` hook can call
241	        it directly. Idempotent: returns ``skipped=True`` if the trial row
242	        already exists server-side (another hook fired, or a previous
243	        streaming run already persisted this trial).
244	        """
245	        return await self._upload_single_trial(
246	            trial_result=trial_result,
247	            trial_dir=trial_dir,
248	            job_id=job_id,
249	            agent_cache=agent_cache,
250	            model_cache=model_cache,
251	        )
252	
253	    async def finalize_job(
254	        self,
255	        job_dir: Path,
256	        *,
257	        job_result: JobResult,
258	        job_config: JobConfig,
259	    ) -> None:
260	        """Build + upload the job archive and write the completion fields.
261	
262	        Called once at end-of-run (streaming) or after all per-trial uploads
263	        complete (batch `upload_job`). Idempotency at this level is the
264	        caller's responsibility — the idempotent-sweep in :meth:`upload_job`
265	        only calls this when the server row still has ``archive_path=NULL``.
266	        """
267	        log_path: str | None = None
268	        job_log = job_dir / "job.log"
269	        if job_log.exists():
270	            log_path = f"jobs/{job_result.id}/job.log"
271	            await self.storage.upload_large_file(
272	                job_log,
273	                log_path,
274	                content_type="text/plain",
275	            )
276	
277	        archive_path = f"jobs/{job_result.id}/job.tar.gz"
278	        local_archive_path = job_dir / ".harbor-upload" / "job.tar.gz"
279	        upload_url_path = local_archive_path.with_suffix(
280	            local_archive_path.suffix + ".tus-url"
281	        )
282	        if not (local_archive_path.exists() and upload_url_path.exists()):
283	            upload_url_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
284	            _create_job_archive_file(job_dir, local_archive_path)
285	        await self.storage.upload_large_file(
286	            local_archive_path,
287	            archive_path,
288	            content_type="application/gzip",
289	        )
290	
291	        await self.db.finalize_job(
292	            job_result.id,
293	            archive_path=archive_path,
294	            log_path=log_path,
295	            finished_at=job_result.finished_at or datetime.now(),
296	        )
297	        local_archive_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
298	        upload_url_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
299	        try:
300	            local_archive_path.parent.rmdir()
301	        except OSError:
302	            pass
303	
304	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
305	    # Top-level orchestrator (idempotent sweep)
306	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
307	
308	    async def upload_job(
309	        self,
310	        job_dir: Path,
311	        *,
312	        visibility: PublicJobVisibility | None = None,
313	        share_orgs: list[str] | None = None,
314	        share_users: list[str] | None = None,
315	        confirm_non_member_orgs: bool = False,
316	        max_concurrency: int = 10,
317	        on_trial_start: Callable[[TrialResult], None] | None = None,
318	        on_trial_complete: Callable[[TrialResult, TrialUploadResult], None]
319	        | None = None,
320	    ) -> JobUploadResult:
321	        """Upload a ``job_dir`` to Supabase — idempotent end-to-end.
322	
323	        This is the public entry point for both:
324	
325	        * **First-time upload** (standalone ``harbor upload``) — inserts the
326	          row, uploads every trial, uploads the job archive, finalizes.
327	        * **Resume after crash / streaming finalize** — detects existing
328	          server state, uploads only the trials that aren't already there,
329	          finalizes only if the row's ``archive_path`` is still ``NULL``.
330	
331	        Re-running always converges to "complete upload."
332	
333	        Visibility semantics (unchanged):
334	        * ``visibility=None`` → new jobs default to private; existing untouched.
335	        * ``visibility="public"``/``"private"`` → always applied, updated if
336	          the existing row's value differs.
337	        """
338	        t0 = time.monotonic()
339	
340	        job_result, job_config, trial_results, trial_dirs = _load_job_from_disk(job_dir)
341	
342	        start = await self.start_job(
343	            job_id=job_result.id,
344	            job_name=job_config.job_name,
345	            started_at=job_result.started_at,
346	            config=job_config.model_dump(mode="json"),
347	            visibility=visibility,
348	            share_orgs=share_orgs,
349	            share_users=share_users,
350	            confirm_non_member_orgs=confirm_non_member_orgs,
351	            n_planned_trials=job_result.n_total_trials,
352	        )
353	
354	        # Eagerly upsert agents + models on the batch path. Streaming relies
355	        # on the per-trial lazy upsert in `_upload_single_trial`; here we
356	        # know the full trial set up front so this saves N round-trips on
357	        # the hot path.
358	        agent_cache, model_cache = await self._upsert_dimensions(trial_results)
359	        start.agent_cache.update(agent_cache)
360	        start.model_cache.update(model_cache)
361	
362	        # Resume path: skip trials that already exist on the server so a
363	        # re-run after a partial streaming upload doesn't double-write.
364	        existing_trial_ids: set[UUID] = set()
365	        if start.already_existed:
366	            existing_trial_ids = await self.db.list_trial_ids_for_job(start.job_id)
367	
368	        # Concurrent per-trial uploads. The `_upload_single_trial` body
369	        # double-checks existence via `trial_exists` so two concurrent
370	        # resume flows don't collide — the list-then-upload gap is purely
371	        # an optimization, not a correctness boundary.
372	        sem = asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrency)
373	
374	        async def _upload_trial(tr: TrialResult) -> TrialUploadResult:
375	            async with sem:
376	                if on_trial_start:
377	                    on_trial_start(tr)
378	                if tr.id in existing_trial_ids:
379	                    result = TrialUploadResult(
380	                        trial_name=tr.trial_name,
381	                        task_name=tr.task_name,
382	                        reward=_extract_primary_reward(tr),
383	                        skipped=True,
384	                    )
385	                else:
386	                    result = await self._upload_single_trial(
387	                        trial_result=tr,
388	                        trial_dir=trial_dirs.get(tr.trial_name),
389	                        job_id=start.job_id,
390	                        agent_cache=start.agent_cache,
391	                        model_cache=start.model_cache,
392	                    )
393	                if on_trial_complete:
394	                    on_trial_complete(tr, result)
395	                return result
396	
397	        async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as tg:
398	            tasks = [tg.create_task(_upload_trial(tr)) for tr in trial_results]
399	
400	        trial_upload_results = [t.result() for t in tasks]
401	
402	        # Finalize only if the server row doesn't already have a job archive.
403	        # Covers: fresh upload (always finalize) + streaming finalize (row
404	        # exists with archive_path=NULL → finalize) + completed re-upload
405	        # (row exists with archive_path set → skip).
406	        remote = await self.db.get_job(start.job_id)
407	        if remote is not None and remote.get("archive_path") is None:
408	            await self.finalize_job(
409	                job_dir, job_result=job_result, job_config=job_config
410	            )
411	
412	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
413	        return JobUploadResult(
414	            job_name=job_config.job_name,
415	            job_id=str(start.job_id),
416	            visibility=start.visibility,
417	            shared_orgs=start.shared_orgs,
418	            shared_users=start.shared_users,
419	            job_already_existed=start.already_existed,
420	            n_trials_uploaded=sum(
421	                1 for r in trial_upload_results if not r.skipped and r.error is None
422	            ),
423	            n_trials_skipped=sum(1 for r in trial_upload_results if r.skipped),
424	            n_trials_failed=sum(1 for r in trial_upload_results if r.error is not None),
425	            total_time_sec=elapsed,
426	            trial_results=trial_upload_results,
427	        )
428	
429	    async def _upsert_dimensions(
430	        self, trial_results: list[TrialResult]
431	    ) -> tuple[
432	        dict[tuple[str, str], str],
433	        dict[tuple[str, str | None], str],
434	    ]:
435	        """Eagerly upsert every agent + model referenced by these trials.
436	
437	        Returns caches keyed by identity tuples so the per-trial upload path
438	        can skip the lookup on the hot path. Model cache key preserves
439	        ``provider=None`` as a distinct identity (see ``JobStartResult``).
440	        """
441	        agent_cache: dict[tuple[str, str], str] = {}
442	        model_cache: dict[tuple[str, str | None], str] = {}
443	        for tr in trial_results:
444	            agent_key = (tr.agent_info.name, tr.agent_info.version)
445	            if agent_key not in agent_cache:
446	                agent_cache[agent_key] = await self.db.upsert_agent(
447	                    tr.agent_info.name, tr.agent_info.version
448	                )
449	            if tr.agent_info.model_info is not None:
450	                model_key = (
451	                    tr.agent_info.model_info.name,
452	                    tr.agent_info.model_info.provider,
453	                )
454	                if model_key not in model_cache:
455	                    model_cache[model_key] = await self.db.upsert_model(
456	                        tr.agent_info.model_info.name,
457	                        tr.agent_info.model_info.provider,
458	                    )
459	        return agent_cache, model_cache
460	
461	    async def _upload_single_trial(
462	        self,
463	        *,
464	        trial_result: TrialResult,
465	        trial_dir: Path | None,
466	        job_id: UUID,
467	        agent_cache: dict[tuple[str, str], str],
468	        model_cache: dict[tuple[str, str | None], str],
469	    ) -> TrialUploadResult:
470	        primary_reward = _extract_primary_reward(trial_result)
471	
472	        try:
473	            # Idempotency: skip if trial already uploaded
474	            if await self.db.trial_exists(trial_result.id):
475	                return TrialUploadResult(
476	                    trial_name=trial_result.trial_name,
477	                    task_name=trial_result.task_name,
478	                    reward=primary_reward,
479	                    skipped=True,
480	                )
481	
482	            t0 = time.monotonic()
483	            archive_path: str | None = None
484	            trajectory_path: str | None = None
485	            archive_size = 0
486	
487	            if trial_dir is not None:
488	                # Create and upload trial archive
489	                archive_bytes = _create_trial_archive(trial_dir)
490	                archive_size = len(archive_bytes)
491	                archive_path = f"trials/{trial_result.id}/trial.tar.gz"
492	                await self.storage.upload_bytes(archive_bytes, archive_path)
493	
494	                # Upload trajectory separately for direct access
495	                traj_file = trial_dir / "agent" / "trajectory.json"
496	                if traj_file.exists():
497	                    direct_trajectory_path = f"trials/{trial_result.id}/trajectory.json"
498	                    try:
499	                        await self.storage.upload_file(
500	                            traj_file, direct_trajectory_path
501	                        )
502	                    except Exception as exc:
503	                        # The trial archive already contains agent/trajectory.json,
504	                        # so a failed direct upload should degrade access to the
505	                        # convenience path rather than poison the whole trial upload.
506	                        logger.debug(
507	                            "Failed to upload direct trajectory for trial %s; "
508	                            "continuing with archive only: %s: %s",
509	                            trial_result.trial_name,
510	                            type(exc).__name__,
511	                            exc,
512	                        )
513	                    else:
514	                        trajectory_path = direct_trajectory_path
515	
516	            # Upsert the agent if we haven't seen this (name, version) pair
517	            # yet. This lazy path matters for the streaming flow — hooks see
518	            # trials one at a time and the cache is initially empty; the
519	            # batch flow pre-populates the cache via _upsert_dimensions.
520	            agent_key = (
521	                trial_result.agent_info.name,
522	                trial_result.agent_info.version,
523	            )
524	            async with self._agent_cache_lock:
525	                if agent_key not in agent_cache:
526	                    agent_cache[agent_key] = await self.db.upsert_agent(
527	                        trial_result.agent_info.name, trial_result.agent_info.version
528	                    )
529	                agent_id = agent_cache[agent_key]
530	
531	            await self.db.insert_trial(
532	                id=trial_result.id,
533	                trial_name=trial_result.trial_name,
534	                task_name=trial_result.task_name,
535	                task_content_hash=trial_result.task_checksum,
536	                job_id=job_id,
537	                agent_id=agent_id,
538	                started_at=trial_result.started_at,
539	                finished_at=trial_result.finished_at,
540	                config=trial_result.config.model_dump(mode="json"),
541	                rewards=(
542	                    trial_result.verifier_result.rewards
543	                    if trial_result.verifier_result
544	                    else None
545	                ),
546	                exception_type=(
547	                    trial_result.exception_info.exception_type
548	                    if trial_result.exception_info
549	                    else None
550	                ),
551	                archive_path=archive_path,
552	                trajectory_path=trajectory_path,
553	                environment_setup_started_at=_timing_field(
554	                    trial_result.environment_setup, "started_at"
555	                ),
556	                environment_setup_finished_at=_timing_field(
557	                    trial_result.environment_setup, "finished_at"
558	                ),
559	                agent_setup_started_at=_timing_field(
560	                    trial_result.agent_setup, "started_at"
561	                ),
562	                agent_setup_finished_at=_timing_field(
563	                    trial_result.agent_setup, "finished_at"
564	                ),
565	                agent_execution_started_at=_timing_field(
566	                    trial_result.agent_execution, "started_at"
567	                ),
568	                agent_execution_finished_at=_timing_field(
569	                    trial_result.agent_execution, "finished_at"
570	                ),
571	                verifier_started_at=_timing_field(trial_result.verifier, "started_at"),
572	                verifier_finished_at=_timing_field(
573	                    trial_result.verifier, "finished_at"
574	                ),
575	            )
576	
577	            # Insert trial_model row if model info and token data available.
578	            # Same lazy-upsert pattern as agent above.
579	            if trial_result.agent_info.model_info is not None:
580	                model_key = (
581	                    trial_result.agent_info.model_info.name,
582	                    trial_result.agent_info.model_info.provider,
583	                )
584	                async with self._model_cache_lock:
585	                    if model_key not in model_cache:
586	                        model_cache[model_key] = await self.db.upsert_model(
587	                            trial_result.agent_info.model_info.name,
588	                            trial_result.agent_info.model_info.provider,
589	                        )
590	                    model_id = model_cache[model_key]
591	
592	                agent_result = trial_result.agent_result
593	                await self.db.insert_trial_model(
594	                    trial_id=trial_result.id,
595	                    model_id=model_id,
596	                    n_input_tokens=(
597	                        agent_result.n_input_tokens if agent_result else None
598	                    ),
599	                    n_cache_tokens=(
600	                        agent_result.n_cache_tokens if agent_result else None
601	                    ),
602	                    n_output_tokens=(
603	                        agent_result.n_output_tokens if agent_result else None
604	                    ),
605	                    cost_usd=agent_result.cost_usd if agent_result else None,
606	                )
607	
608	            elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
609	            return TrialUploadResult(
610	                trial_name=trial_result.trial_name,
611	                task_name=trial_result.task_name,
612	                reward=primary_reward,
613	                archive_size_bytes=archive_size,
614	                upload_time_sec=elapsed,
615	            )
616	
617	        except Exception as exc:
618	            logger.debug("Failed to upload trial %s: %s", trial_result.trial_name, exc)
619	            return TrialUploadResult(
620	                trial_name=trial_result.trial_name,
621	                task_name=trial_result.task_name,
622	                reward=primary_reward,
623	                error=f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
624	            )
625	
626	
627	def _load_job_from_disk(
628	    job_dir: Path,
629	) -> tuple[JobResult, JobConfig, list[TrialResult], dict[str, Path]]:
630	    """Load the job_dir's result.json + config.json + every trial's result.json.
631	
632	    Returns the parent result/config plus a parallel list of child trial
633	    results and a name→path map the uploader uses to find each trial's
634	    on-disk subdir. Used by both ``start_job`` and ``upload_job`` — kept as
635	    a single helper so both paths load the same trial set with the same
636	    ordering and detection rules.
637	    """
638	    job_result = JobResult.model_validate_json((job_dir / "result.json").read_text())
639	    job_config = JobConfig.model_validate_json((job_dir / "config.json").read_text())
640	    trial_dirs: dict[str, Path] = {}
641	    trial_results: list[TrialResult] = []
642	    for child in sorted(job_dir.iterdir()):
643	        if child.is_dir() and (child / "result.json").exists():
644	            trial_dirs[child.name] = child
645	            trial_results.append(
646	                TrialResult.model_validate_json((child / "result.json").read_text())
647	            )
648	    return job_result, job_config, trial_results, trial_dirs
649	
650	
651	def _timing_field(timing: object | None, field: str) -> datetime | None:
652	    if timing is None:
653	        return None
654	    value = getattr(timing, field, None)
655	    if isinstance(value, datetime):
656	        return value
657	    return None
658	
659	
660	def _extract_primary_reward(trial_result: TrialResult) -> float | int | None:
661	    if (
662	        trial_result.verifier_result is not None
663	        and trial_result.verifier_result.rewards
664	    ):
665	        rewards = trial_result.verifier_result.rewards
666	        if "reward" in rewards:
667	            return rewards["reward"]
668	        if len(rewards) == 1:
669	            return next(iter(rewards.values()))
670	    return None
671	
672	
673	def _create_trial_archive(trial_dir: Path) -> bytes:
674	    """Create a tar.gz archive of the allowlisted entries in a trial dir.
675	
676	    Only entries in ``_TRIAL_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES`` are included. Missing
677	    optional entries (e.g. ``exception.txt`` when the trial didn't fail,
678	    ``analysis.md`` when no analysis was written) are silently skipped.
679	    Entries live at the root of the archive so extraction into an empty
680	    ``trial_dir`` restores the original layout.
681	    """
682	    buf = BytesIO()
683	    with tarfile.open(fileobj=buf, mode="w:gz") as tar:
684	        _add_trial_archive_entries(tar, trial_dir)
685	    return buf.getvalue()
686	
687	
688	def _add_trial_archive_entries(
689	    tar: tarfile.TarFile, trial_dir: Path, *, arcname_prefix: str = ""
690	) -> None:
691	    """Add allowlisted trial entries to an open tar archive."""
692	    for name in _TRIAL_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES:
693	        path = trial_dir / name
694	        if path.exists():
695	            tar.add(path, arcname=f"{arcname_prefix}{name}")
696	
697	    steps_dir = trial_dir / "steps"
698	    if not steps_dir.exists():
699	        return
700	
701	    for step_dir in sorted(p for p in steps_dir.iterdir() if p.is_dir()):
702	        for name in _STEP_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES:
703	            path = step_dir / name
704	            if path.exists():
705	                tar.add(
706	                    path,
707	                    arcname=f"{arcname_prefix}steps/{step_dir.name}/{name}",
708	                )
709	
710	
711	def _create_job_archive(job_dir: Path) -> bytes:
712	    """Create a tar.gz archive of a job_dir: top-level allowlist + trial subdirs.
713	
714	    Top-level files from ``_JOB_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES`` are included. Then every
715	    child directory with a ``result.json`` inside is treated as a trial
716	    subdir, and its contents are filtered through ``_TRIAL_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES``
717	    (same detection rule ``Uploader.upload_job`` uses to enumerate trials).
718	    Entries are rooted at ``{job_name}/...`` so ``extractall`` into an
719	    ``output_dir`` produces ``output_dir/{job_name}/...``.
720	    """
721	    buf = BytesIO()
722	    with tarfile.open(fileobj=buf, mode="w:gz") as tar:
723	        _add_job_archive_entries(tar, job_dir)
724	    return buf.getvalue()
725	
726	
727	def _create_job_archive_file(job_dir: Path, archive_path: Path) -> None:
728	    """Create the job archive on disk to avoid holding large jobs in memory."""
729	    archive_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
730	    with tarfile.open(archive_path, mode="w:gz") as tar:
731	        _add_job_archive_entries(tar, job_dir)
732	
733	
734	def _add_job_archive_entries(tar: tarfile.TarFile, job_dir: Path) -> None:
735	    root = job_dir.name
736	    for name in _JOB_ARCHIVE_INCLUDES:
737	        path = job_dir / name
738	        if path.exists():
739	            tar.add(path, arcname=f"{root}/{name}")
740	    for child in sorted(job_dir.iterdir()):
741	        if not (child.is_dir() and (child / "result.json").exists()):
742	            continue
743	        _add_trial_archive_entries(
744	            tar,
745	            child,
746	            arcname_prefix=f"{root}/{child.name}/",
747	        )
748
